Description: Detected a Generic API Key, potentially exposing access to various services and sensitive operations.
Author: Tiago Rosado
Message:
fix(pki): compose-path bootstrap_token SHA-256 mismatch — host-side manifest update

Root cause (compose path):
  The PKI issuer container runs bootstrap() which writes fresh bootstrap tokens
  to /secrets/<svc>_bootstrap_token and calls _update_manifest_hashes() to
  write the corresponding SHA-256 values back into /manifest.yaml inside the
  container.  On macOS Podman (remote client to applehv VM), the subsequent
  podman cp "${_cname}:/manifest.yaml" copy-back silently fails to land on
  the host filesystem — the host docker/service_identities.yaml retains the
  stale committed hashes from a previous install.  On every subsequent fresh
  install the pattern repeats: new tokens generated, manifest updated in
  container, copy-back fails, host manifest stays stale.  Gateway and
  backoffice then start with on-disk tokens that don't match the stale
  manifest hashes → TamperError → OPA unreachable → fail-closed 502.

  Root-cause evidence: install.log shows manifest write-back event with the
  same pre_sha256=884b3b0a across 9+ consecutive fresh installs; host file
  mtime never advances beyond the last git pull.  podman cp verified working
  in isolation (fresh bash subprocess test) but does not land during the
  install.sh function (likely a Podman remote client flush ordering issue).

Fix 1 — host-side manifest update in _pki_run_issuer_podman_macos (install.sh):
  After podman cp copies the *_bootstrap_token files back to the host
  (docker/secrets/), a Python 3 one-liner re-derives SHA-256 from each
  on-disk token file and patches service_identities.yaml directly.  This
  bypasses the unreliable container-side copy-back of the manifest and is
  authoritative regardless of whether the container's cp-back landed.
  Python 3 is guaranteed on macOS 12+ (same requirement as the install).

Fix 2 — _update_manifest_hashes in rotate_leaves() (issuer.py):
  rotate_leaves() called _ensure_bootstrap_token() but discarded the hash
  and never called _update_manifest_hashes().  On the compose upgrade path
  (ca_root.crt already exists → rotate-leaves not bootstrap), the manifest
  hashes were never updated → same TamperError on first restart after
  cert rotation.  Fix: collect hashes into hashes_by_service{} and call
  _update_manifest_hashes() at the end of rotate_leaves(), matching the
  bootstrap() flow.  rotate_intermediate() and rotate_root() call
  rotate_leaves() / bootstrap() so they inherit the fix.  K8s is
  unaffected (token verification skipped via _IN_KUBERNETES from 83556b5).

Consumer enumeration (per feedback_enumerate_before_edit.md):
  grep -rn "bootstrap_token" install.sh src/ docker/ helm/:
    install.sh:5888,5911,5958,5970,6568-6575 — PKI chown + write-back comments
    install.sh:_pki_run_issuer_podman_macos — host-side update added HERE
    src/yashigani/pki/issuer.py:rotate_leaves — _update_manifest_hashes added HERE
    src/yashigani/pki/issuer.py:bootstrap — already correct (no change)
    src/yashigani/pki/identity.py:_verify_bootstrap_token — unchanged
    docker/service_identities.yaml — hashes updated to match current install
    helm/yashigani/files/service_identities.yaml — unchanged (K8s skips verify)

  docker/service_identities.yaml hashes updated to reflect the running install
  (2026-05-17 08:09 BST) tokens.  These are install-specific and will be
  overwritten by install.sh on every fresh install; committed values serve as
  "last dev-install" documentation.

Regression test: test_rotate_leaves_updates_manifest_hashes (test_pki_issuer.py)
  Fails on the pre-fix rotate_leaves() because _update_manifest_hashes is not
  called; passes after the fix.  Checks that the on-disk token SHA matches
  the manifest hash after rotation.

Verification on Track B (current running stack, macOS Podman):
  * Ran host-side Python snippet directly → 26 service hashes updated
  * Gateway restarted → no bootstrap token mismatch errors (--since 5m clean)
  * OPA calls: POST https://policy:8181/v1/data/yashigani/v1/decision HTTP 200
  * Ollama model load fails (OOM — VM has 56 MB free with 26 containers):
    LLM round-trip blocked at Ollama layer, NOT at OPA layer.
    OPA path is fixed; Ollama OOM is a separate resource-constraint issue.

All 16 pki_issuer unit tests pass (15 pre-existing + 1 new regression).

Description: Detected a Generic API Key, potentially exposing access to various services and sensitive operations.
Author: Tiago Rosado
Message:
fix(pki): compose-path bootstrap_token SHA-256 mismatch — host-side manifest update

Root cause (compose path):
  The PKI issuer container runs bootstrap() which writes fresh bootstrap tokens
  to /secrets/<svc>_bootstrap_token and calls _update_manifest_hashes() to
  write the corresponding SHA-256 values back into /manifest.yaml inside the
  container.  On macOS Podman (remote client to applehv VM), the subsequent
  podman cp "${_cname}:/manifest.yaml" copy-back silently fails to land on
  the host filesystem — the host docker/service_identities.yaml retains the
  stale committed hashes from a previous install.  On every subsequent fresh
  install the pattern repeats: new tokens generated, manifest updated in
  container, copy-back fails, host manifest stays stale.  Gateway and
  backoffice then start with on-disk tokens that don't match the stale
  manifest hashes → TamperError → OPA unreachable → fail-closed 502.

  Root-cause evidence: install.log shows manifest write-back event with the
  same pre_sha256=884b3b0a across 9+ consecutive fresh installs; host file
  mtime never advances beyond the last git pull.  podman cp verified working
  in isolation (fresh bash subprocess test) but does not land during the
  install.sh function (likely a Podman remote client flush ordering issue).

Description: Detected a Generic API Key, potentially exposing access to various services and sensitive operations.
Author: Tiago Rosado
Message:
fix(pki): compose-path bootstrap_token SHA-256 mismatch — host-side manifest update

Root cause (compose path):
  The PKI issuer container runs bootstrap() which writes fresh bootstrap tokens
  to /secrets/<svc>_bootstrap_token and calls _update_manifest_hashes() to
  write the corresponding SHA-256 values back into /manifest.yaml inside the
  container.  On macOS Podman (remote client to applehv VM), the subsequent
  podman cp "${_cname}:/manifest.yaml" copy-back silently fails to land on
  the host filesystem — the host docker/service_identities.yaml retains the
  stale committed hashes from a previous install.  On every subsequent fresh
  install the pattern repeats: new tokens generated, manifest updated in
  container, copy-back fails, host manifest stays stale.  Gateway and
  backoffice then start with on-disk tokens that don't match the stale
  manifest hashes → TamperError → OPA unreachable → fail-closed 502.

  Root-cause evidence: install.log shows manifest write-back event with the
  same pre_sha256=884b3b0a across 9+ consecutive fresh installs; host file
  mtime never advances beyond the last git pull.  podman cp verified working
  in isolation (fresh bash subprocess test) but does not land during the
  install.sh function (likely a Podman remote client flush ordering issue).

Fix 1 — host-side manifest update in _pki_run_issuer_podman_macos (install.sh):
  After podman cp copies the *_bootstrap_token files back to the host
  (docker/secrets/), a Python 3 one-liner re-derives SHA-256 from each
  on-disk token file and patches service_identities.yaml directly.  This
  bypasses the unreliable container-side copy-back of the manifest and is
  authoritative regardless of whether the container's cp-back landed.
  Python 3 is guaranteed on macOS 12+ (same requirement as the install).

Fix 2 — _update_manifest_hashes in rotate_leaves() (issuer.py):
  rotate_leaves() called _ensure_bootstrap_token() but discarded the hash
  and never called _update_manifest_hashes().  On the compose upgrade path
  (ca_root.crt already exists → rotate-leaves not bootstrap), the manifest
  hashes were never updated → same TamperError on first restart after
  cert rotation.  Fix: collect hashes into hashes_by_service{} and call
  _update_manifest_hashes() at the end of rotate_leaves(), matching the
  bootstrap() flow.  rotate_intermediate() and rotate_root() call
  rotate_leaves() / bootstrap() so they inherit the fix.  K8s is
  unaffected (token verification skipped via _IN_KUBERNETES from 83556b5).

Description: Detected a Generic API Key, potentially exposing access to various services and sensitive operations.
Author: Tiago Rosado
Message:
fix(pki): compose-path bootstrap_token SHA-256 mismatch — host-side manifest update

Root cause (compose path):
  The PKI issuer container runs bootstrap() which writes fresh bootstrap tokens
  to /secrets/<svc>_bootstrap_token and calls _update_manifest_hashes() to
  write the corresponding SHA-256 values back into /manifest.yaml inside the
  container.  On macOS Podman (remote client to applehv VM), the subsequent
  podman cp "${_cname}:/manifest.yaml" copy-back silently fails to land on
  the host filesystem — the host docker/service_identities.yaml retains the
  stale committed hashes from a previous install.  On every subsequent fresh
  install the pattern repeats: new tokens generated, manifest updated in
  container, copy-back fails, host manifest stays stale.  Gateway and
  backoffice then start with on-disk tokens that don't match the stale
  manifest hashes → TamperError → OPA unreachable → fail-closed 502.

  Root-cause evidence: install.log shows manifest write-back event with the
  same pre_sha256=884b3b0a across 9+ consecutive fresh installs; host file
  mtime never advances beyond the last git pull.  podman cp verified working
  in isolation (fresh bash subprocess test) but does not land during the
  install.sh function (likely a Podman remote client flush ordering issue).

Fix 1 — host-side manifest update in _pki_run_issuer_podman_macos (install.sh):
  After podman cp copies the *_bootstrap_token files back to the host
  (docker/secrets/), a Python 3 one-liner re-derives SHA-256 from each
  on-disk token file and patches service_identities.yaml directly.  This
  bypasses the unreliable container-side copy-back of the manifest and is
  authoritative regardless of whether the container's cp-back landed.
  Python 3 is guaranteed on macOS 12+ (same requirement as the install).

Fix 2 — _update_manifest_hashes in rotate_leaves() (issuer.py):
  rotate_leaves() called _ensure_bootstrap_token() but discarded the hash
  and never called _update_manifest_hashes().  On the compose upgrade path
  (ca_root.crt already exists → rotate-leaves not bootstrap), the manifest
  hashes were never updated → same TamperError on first restart after
  cert rotation.  Fix: collect hashes into hashes_by_service{} and call
  _update_manifest_hashes() at the end of rotate_leaves(), matching the
  bootstrap() flow.  rotate_intermediate() and rotate_root() call
  rotate_leaves() / bootstrap() so they inherit the fix.  K8s is
  unaffected (token verification skipped via _IN_KUBERNETES from 83556b5).

Consumer enumeration (per feedback_enumerate_before_edit.md):

    docker/service_identities.yaml — hashes updated to match current install
    helm/yashigani/files/service_identities.yaml — unchanged (K8s skips verify)

  docker/service_identities.yaml hashes updated to reflect the running install

  * Ollama model load fails (OOM — VM has 56 MB free with 26 containers):
    LLM round-trip blocked at Ollama layer, NOT at OPA layer.
    OPA path is fixed; Ollama OOM is a separate resource-constraint issue.

All 16 pki_issuer unit tests pass (15 pre-existing + 1 new regression).

Description: Detected a Generic API Key, potentially exposing access to various services and sensitive operations.
Author: Tiago Rosado
Message:
fix(pki): compose-path bootstrap_token SHA-256 mismatch — host-side manifest update

Root cause (compose path):
  The PKI issuer container runs bootstrap() which writes fresh bootstrap tokens
  to /secrets/<svc>_bootstrap_token and calls _update_manifest_hashes() to
  write the corresponding SHA-256 values back into /manifest.yaml inside the
  container.  On macOS Podman (remote client to applehv VM), the subsequent
  podman cp "${_cname}:/manifest.yaml" copy-back silently fails to land on
  the host filesystem — the host docker/service_identities.yaml retains the
  stale committed hashes from a previous install.  On every subsequent fresh
  install the pattern repeats: new tokens generated, manifest updated in
  container, copy-back fails, host manifest stays stale.  Gateway and
  backoffice then start with on-disk tokens that don't match the stale
  manifest hashes → TamperError → OPA unreachable → fail-closed 502.

  Root-cause evidence: install.log shows manifest write-back event with the
  same pre_sha256=884b3b0a across 9+ consecutive fresh installs; host file
  mtime never advances beyond the last git pull.  podman cp verified working
  in isolation (fresh bash subprocess test) but does not land during the
  install.sh function (likely a Podman remote client flush ordering issue).

Fix 1 — host-side manifest update in _pki_run_issuer_podman_macos (install.sh):
  After podman cp copies the *_bootstrap_token files back to the host
  (docker/secrets/), a Python 3 one-liner re-derives SHA-256 from each
  on-disk token file and patches service_identities.yaml directly.  This
  bypasses the unreliable container-side copy-back of the manifest and is
  authoritative regardless of whether the container's cp-back landed.
  Python 3 is guaranteed on macOS 12+ (same requirement as the install).

Fix 2 — _update_manifest_hashes in rotate_leaves() (issuer.py):
  rotate_leaves() called _ensure_bootstrap_token() but discarded the hash
  and never called _update_manifest_hashes().  On the compose upgrade path
  (ca_root.crt already exists → rotate-leaves not bootstrap), the manifest
  hashes were never updated → same TamperError on first restart after
  cert rotation.  Fix: collect hashes into hashes_by_service{} and call
  _update_manifest_hashes() at the end of rotate_leaves(), matching the
  bootstrap() flow.  rotate_intermediate() and rotate_root() call
  rotate_leaves() / bootstrap() so they inherit the fix.  K8s is
  unaffected (token verification skipped via _IN_KUBERNETES from 83556b5).

Consumer enumeration (per feedback_enumerate_before_edit.md):
  grep -rn "bootstrap_token" install.sh src/ docker/ helm/:
    install.sh:5888,5911,5958,5970,6568-6575 — PKI chown + write-back comments
    install.sh:_pki_run_issuer_podman_macos — host-side update added HERE
    src/yashigani/pki/issuer.py:rotate_leaves — _update_manifest_hashes added HERE
    src/yashigani/pki/issuer.py:bootstrap — already correct (no change)
    src/yashigani/pki/identity.py:_verify_bootstrap_token — unchanged
    docker/service_identities.yaml — hashes updated to match current install
    helm/yashigani/files/service_identities.yaml — unchanged (K8s skips verify)

  docker/service_identities.yaml hashes updated to reflect the running install
  (2026-05-17 08:09 BST) tokens.  These are install-specific and will be
  overwritten by install.sh on every fresh install; committed values serve as
  "last dev-install" documentation.

Regression test: test_rotate_leaves_updates_manifest_hashes (test_pki_issuer.py)
  Fails on the pre-fix rotate_leaves() because _update_manifest_hashes is not
  called; passes after the fix.  Checks that the on-disk token SHA matches
  the manifest hash after rotation.

Verification on Track B (current running stack, macOS Podman):
  * Ran host-side Python snippet directly → 26 service hashes updated
  * Gateway restarted → no bootstrap token mismatch errors (--since 5m clean)
  * OPA calls: POST https://policy:8181/v1/data/yashigani/v1/decision HTTP 200
  * Ollama model load fails (OOM — VM has 56 MB free with 26 containers):
    LLM round-trip blocked at Ollama layer, NOT at OPA layer.
    OPA path is fixed; Ollama OOM is a separate resource-constraint issue.

All 16 pki_issuer unit tests pass (15 pre-existing + 1 new regression).

Description: Detected a Generic API Key, potentially exposing access to various services and sensitive operations.
Author: Tiago Rosado
Message:
chore(register): rename risk ID prefix ACS-RISK- → YSG-RISK- everywhere

ACS-RISK-* IDs were misleading: ACS (Agnostic Compliance Scanner) is the
tool that discovers the risks; Yashigani is the product the risks live in.
The prefix biased cognition toward 'ACS owns this risk' when in fact
Yashigani owns it. Tiago directive 2026-05-16: change the IDs.

Sweep applied identically to:
- /Internal/Risk-Register/yashigani-risks.md (canonical register, 159 hits)
- This source tree (compose comments, helm chart, tests, CI workflows, docs)
- Agent-memory references (not in this repo)

Commit history retains the old ACS-RISK-* IDs in past commit messages —
those are immutable. Going forward, all new references use YSG-RISK-*.

## What changed

1. docker/service_identities.yaml — added endpoint_acls["/admin/agents"] with:
   allowed_spiffe_ids:
     - spiffe://yashigani.internal/caddy      # admin UI browser sessions via Caddy TLS leg
     - spiffe://yashigani.internal/backoffice # install.sh agent-reg inside backoffice container

2. src/yashigani/backoffice/routes/agents.py — added:
   - `from fastapi import Depends` (was missing)
   - `from yashigani.auth.spiffe import require_spiffe_id`
   - `dependencies=[Depends(require_spiffe_id("/admin/agents"))]` on 4 mutation routes:
     POST  /admin/agents                     (register_agent)
     PUT   /admin/agents/{agent_id}          (update_agent)
     DELETE /admin/agents/{agent_id}         (deactivate_agent)
     POST  /admin/agents/{agent_id}/token/rotate (rotate_agent_token)
   GET routes (list_agents, get_agent, get_agent_quickstart) are NOT gated — read-only.

3. src/tests/unit/test_acs_risk_012b_spiffe_admin_agents.py — 9 new unit tests:
   - Tests 1-4: missing SPIFFE header on each of the 4 mutation routes → 401 no_spiffe_id
   - Test 5: rogue SPIFFE ID (attacker's own cert) → 403 spiffe_id_not_allowed
   - Test 6: caddy SPIFFE ID passes gate
   - Test 7: backoffice SPIFFE ID (install.sh path) passes gate
   - Test 8: GET /admin/agents has no require_spiffe_id dependency
   - Test 9: service_identities.yaml has /admin/agents ACL with exactly 2 IDs

## Threat model

Stolen admin session cookie replayed directly against backoffice:8443 by attacker
holding their own mTLS cert: peer_cert SPIFFE URI ≠ caddy/backoffice → 403
spiffe_id_not_allowed at application layer before any route handler executes.

Admin UI browser path: browser → Caddy (validates no client cert needed) → Caddy
presents caddy_client.crt to backoffice → SpiffePeerCertMiddleware extracts
x-spiffe-id-peer-cert = spiffe://yashigani.internal/caddy → gate passes.

install.sh path: runs inside backoffice container → localhost:8443 with
backoffice_client.crt → x-spiffe-id-peer-cert = spiffe://yashigani.internal/backoffice → gate passes.

Description: Detected a Generic API Key, potentially exposing access to various services and sensitive operations.
Author: tr
Message:
feat(sso): implement broker SAML branch in add_idp() + entrypoint env-var wiring (D2 — v2.23.4)

Consumers of add_idp / _saml_providers / handle_saml_response / YASHIGANI_IDP_:
  src/yashigani/auth/broker.py:119              — add_idp() [MODIFIED — SAML branch added]
  src/yashigani/auth/broker.py:114,288          — _saml_providers [MODIFIED — now populated]
  src/yashigani/auth/broker.py:271              — handle_saml_response [UNCHANGED]
  src/yashigani/backoffice/entrypoint.py:389    — IdP env-var loop [MODIFIED — SAML vars]
  src/yashigani/backoffice/routes/sso.py:1041   — handle_saml_response call site [UNCHANGED]
  src/tests/unit/test_v6840_acr_amr_saml.py     — broker._saml_providers direct write [UNCHANGED]
  src/tests/unit/test_v2232_cscan_01m_internal_str_exc.py — _saml_providers direct write [UNCHANGED]
  docker/docker-compose.yml:321-336             — YASHIGANI_IDP_* env passthrough [MODIFIED]
  docker/.env.example:72-79                     — YASHIGANI_IDP_* docs [MODIFIED]
  helm/yashigani/values.yaml:1107-1124          — sso.idps example [MODIFIED]

Changes:
- broker.py: IdPConfig gains 6 new SAML fields (saml_idp_sso_url, saml_idp_sls_url,
  saml_idp_x509_cert, saml_sp_sls_url, saml_sp_private_key, saml_sp_certificate).
  add_idp() SAML branch constructs SAMLConfig + SAMLProvider — _assert_rsa_sp_key()
  fires at add_idp() time, failing closed for non-RSA SP keys (ACS-RISK-044).
  remove_idp() now also clears _saml_providers.
- backoffice/entrypoint.py: IdP env-var loop reads YASHIGANI_IDP_<N>_SAML_IDP_SSO_URL,
  _SAML_IDP_SLS_URL, _SAML_IDP_X509_CERT, _SAML_SP_SLS_URL, _SAML_SP_PRIVATE_KEY,
  _SAML_SP_CERT, _ENTITY_ID. Default ACS URL for SAML IdPs is
  https://<domain>/auth/sso/saml/<id>/acs (vs /auth/sso/oidc/<id>/callback for OIDC).
- Tests: 19 new unit tests (B01-B11 + integration) in test_v2234_d2_broker_saml_branch.py.
  Keys generated at test time via cryptography library (RSA-2048 + EC P-256).
  All 2047 unit tests pass.
- docker/.env.example, docker-compose.yml: SAML vars documented and passed through.
- helm/yashigani/values.yaml: SAMLv2 IdP example added to sso.idps comment block.

Env-var naming convention: YASHIGANI_IDP_<N>_SAML_* prefix for SAML-specific fields,
matching the existing YASHIGANI_IDP_<N>_* convention for protocol-agnostic fields.

ACS-RISK-044/045 rationale: PENDING-CVA "NOT-REACHABLE (feature path absent)" is
superseded — rationale updated in risk register (separate commit).

Description: PEM-encoded private key block
Author: tr
Message:
feat(saml): enforce RSA SP key at runtime — ACS-RISK-044 mitigation

Rationale
---------
ACS-RISK-044 (CVE-2026-41989): libgcrypt ECDH heap-buffer-overflow in
gcry_pk_decrypt.  The vulnerable code path is only reachable when the SAML
SP private key is EC-type (ECDH-ES key-transport in an EncryptedAssertion
triggers the ECDH branch in xmlsec1 → libgcrypt).  RSA SP keys route to
RSA decryption and never reach gcry_pk_decrypt.

The standard install.sh generates RSA SP keys (openssl genrsa), so the
deployment default is already safe.  This commit code-enforces the invariant
so an operator cannot accidentally swap in an EC key without receiving a
clear startup error.

Enforcement point
-----------------
src/yashigani/sso/saml.py  _assert_rsa_sp_key()  (line 22)
Called from: SAMLProvider.__init__ (line 134)

Check fires once at provider init time, not on every SAML request.
Fail-closed: non-RSA key → ValueError immediately; provider is not created;
SAML feature cannot be used.  RSA keys proceed as today.

Error message shape
-------------------
  "SAML SP key must be RSA (mitigates ACS-RISK-044 / CVE-2026-41989).
   Got: EllipticCurvePrivateKey.
   EC/EdDSA/DSA SP keys are not permitted — PQR support is deferred.
   Regenerate with: openssl genrsa -out sp_key.pem 4096"

The check handles both python3-saml stripped-body format (no PEM headers)
and full PEM (headers present); PKCS#8 format primary, PKCS#1 RSA fallback.

Tests added
-----------
src/tests/unit/test_v2234_rsa_sp_key_enforcement.py  — 10 tests, all green

  test_rsa_key_accepted                  RSA PKCS#8 body — no raise
  test_ec_key_rejected                   P-256 PKCS#8 body — ValueError

Description: PEM-encoded private key block
Author: tr
Message:
feat(saml): enforce RSA SP key at runtime — ACS-RISK-044 mitigation

Rationale
---------
ACS-RISK-044 (CVE-2026-41989): libgcrypt ECDH heap-buffer-overflow in
gcry_pk_decrypt.  The vulnerable code path is only reachable when the SAML
SP private key is EC-type (ECDH-ES key-transport in an EncryptedAssertion
triggers the ECDH branch in xmlsec1 → libgcrypt).  RSA SP keys route to
RSA decryption and never reach gcry_pk_decrypt.

The standard install.sh generates RSA SP keys (openssl genrsa), so the
deployment default is already safe.  This commit code-enforces the invariant
so an operator cannot accidentally swap in an EC key without receiving a
clear startup error.

Enforcement point
-----------------
src/yashigani/sso/saml.py  _assert_rsa_sp_key()  (line 22)
Called from: SAMLProvider.__init__ (line 134)

Check fires once at provider init time, not on every SAML request.
Fail-closed: non-RSA key → ValueError immediately; provider is not created;
SAML feature cannot be used.  RSA keys proceed as today.

Error message shape
-------------------
  "SAML SP key must be RSA (mitigates ACS-RISK-044 / CVE-2026-41989).
   Got: EllipticCurvePrivateKey.
   EC/EdDSA/DSA SP keys are not permitted — PQR support is deferred.
   Regenerate with: openssl genrsa -out sp_key.pem 4096"

The check handles both python3-saml stripped-body format (no PEM headers)
and full PEM (headers present); PKCS#8 format primary, PKCS#1 RSA fallback.

Tests added
-----------
src/tests/unit/test_v2234_rsa_sp_key_enforcement.py  — 10 tests, all green

  test_rsa_key_accepted                  RSA PKCS#8 body — no raise
  test_ec_key_rejected                   P-256 PKCS#8 body — ValueError
  test_dsa_key_rejected                  DSA PKCS#8 body — ValueError
  test_error_message_cites_acs_risk_044  message contains "ACS-RISK-044"
  test_error_message_cites_remediation   message contains "openssl genrsa"
  test_rsa_full_pem_accepted             full RSA PEM (with headers) — no raise
  test_ec_full_pem_rejected              full EC PEM (with headers) — ValueError
  test_saml_provider_init_rsa_succeeds   SAMLProvider.__init__ RSA path — OK
  test_saml_provider_init_ec_raises      SAMLProvider.__init__ EC path — ValueError
  test_saml_provider_init_dsa_raises     SAMLProvider.__init__ DSA path — ValueError

grep consumers of _assert_rsa_sp_key / SAMLProvider.__init__:
  src/yashigani/sso/saml.py:134           definition + call site

Description: Detected a Generic API Key, potentially exposing access to various services and sensitive operations.
Author: tr
Message:
feat(auth): /me/api-key self-service Bearer issuance — Gap 4 / v2.23.4 arch-completion

New routes:
  POST   /me/api-key              — issue/rotate HUMAN-identity Bearer (StepUp required)
  GET    /me/api-keys             — list key metadata, NO plaintext, never re-fetchable
  DELETE /me/api-keys/{key_id}   — revoke a key; 403 on cross-user attempt
  POST   /admin/users/{username}/api-key — admin override; 30-sec grace on prior token

Security invariants:
  - Plaintext returned ONCE in POST body only; never logged; never via GET
  - Self-rotation: grace_seconds=0 — prior token immediately invalidated
  - Admin rotation: grace_seconds=30 — mirrors agents/token_rotation.py pattern
  - Rate-limit: 5 issuance attempts per user per hour (Redis INCR/EXPIRE)
  - account_tier == "user" guard on all /me/* routes — admin + totp_provisioning rejected
  - identity_registry is None → 503 (community-tier graceful error)
  - force_password_change/force_totp_provision → 403 (partially-provisioned block)

New module: src/yashigani/backoffice/routes/me.py (~230 LOC)
Changed:    src/yashigani/backoffice/routes/users.py (+80 LOC, admin override route)
Changed:    src/yashigani/audit/schema.py (+60 LOC: UserApiKeyIssuedEvent,
            UserApiKeyRevokedEvent, AdminUserApiKeyIssuedEvent + EventType entries)
Changed:    src/yashigani/backoffice/routes/__init__.py (me_router barrel export)
Changed:    src/yashigani/backoffice/app.py (me_router include_router wiring)
New test:   src/tests/unit/test_v2234_gap4_me_api_key.py (22 tests, all green)

Rate-limiter mechanism: Redis INCR + EXPIRE on key me:api-key:rl:{account_id}
— consistent with _record_auth_failure / _apply_auth_throttle pattern in auth.py.

Grace-window mechanism: registry.rotate_key(identity_id, grace_seconds=30)
— identity:key:grace:{identity_id} set with TTL=30. Mirrors token_rotation.py
which uses grace_period_hours (scaled to seconds for 30-sec transition window).

Identity lookup: _resolve_email_slug() → _auth_email_to_slug() (same as Gap 3).
Admin audit trail: AdminUserApiKeyIssuedEvent with actor, target, key_last4,
grace_seconds — satisfies Lu's Gap 4 audit-trail requirement.

Grep consumers of new routes (all consumers enumerated per SOP 0):
  src/yashigani/backoffice/routes/__init__.py:me_router export
  src/yashigani/backoffice/app.py:app.include_router(me_router, tags=["me"])
  src/yashigani/backoffice/routes/me.py:router (defines all /me/* routes)
  src/yashigani/backoffice/routes/users.py:/{username}/api-key (admin override)
  src/tests/unit/test_v2234_gap4_me_api_key.py:22 regression tests

Confidence: plaintext-via-GET regression caught by test 1 + test 7; tier-bypass
regression caught by tests 3+4; grace_seconds regression caught by tests 6+11.

Description: PEM-encoded private key block
Author: Agnostic Security
Message:
feat(pki): admin UI + BYO-CA driver (#51 + #53) (#111)

Closes issue #51 (PKI admin UI) and issue #53 (BYO-CA driver) as a single
bundled PR on feat/v233-pki-bundle from origin/v2.23.3 base (624cdb5).

## New modules

Driver abstraction:
  src/yashigani/pki/drivers/__init__.py   — barrel: CADriver, CertChainInfo,
                                            DriverError, RotateResult,
                                            InternalCADriver, ByoCADriver
  src/yashigani/pki/drivers/base.py       — CADriver ABC + dataclasses
  src/yashigani/pki/drivers/internal_ca.py — wraps yashigani.pki.issuer;
                                              YASHIGANI_SECRETS_DIR + manifest
  src/yashigani/pki/drivers/byo_ca.py    — BYO-CA: EC P-256 CSR → HTTPS
                                            signing endpoint → atomic install
  src/yashigani/pki/driver_factory.py    — get_ca_driver(): env var → instance

API routes:
  src/yashigani/backoffice/routes/pki_v1.py — /api/v1/admin/pki/*

Admin UI:
  src/yashigani/backoffice/static/js/pki.js — CSP-compliant external JS

## API surface

  GET  /api/v1/admin/pki/chain/{service}   AdminSession
  POST /api/v1/admin/pki/rotate/{service}  StepUpAdminSession (ASVS V6.8.4)
  GET  /api/v1/admin/pki/bundle/{service}  AdminSession (CWE-200: no private key)
  GET  /api/v1/admin/pki/status            AdminSession

## Security invariants

- Private key never in any API response: explicit PRIVATE KEY byte check before
  streaming bundle (CWE-200).
- Service name regex [a-z][a-z0-9_\-]{0,63}$ — path traversal prevention.
- Step-up TOTP required for rotation (ASVS V6.8.4).
- Body limit 256 bytes (ASVS 4.3.1).
- Audit events: PKI_CERT_ROTATED, PKI_CERT_ROTATION_FAILED.
- BYO-CA fail-closed: DriverError on any signing failure; no silent fallback.
- BYO-CA key write is atomic (tempfile + Path.replace()).
- BYO-CA chain validation: leaf.issuer == BYO CA subject.

## Tests

  src/tests/unit/test_pki_driver.py       — 12 tests PKI-D-01…12
  src/tests/unit/test_pki_v1_routes.py    — 11 tests PKI-R-01…11
  src/tests/playwright/test_pki_admin_ui.py — 10 tests PW-PKI-01…10

23/23 unit tests passing. Ruff clean. Mypy clean (5 source files, 0 errors).

## Modified files

  src/yashigani/backoffice/routes/__init__.py — export pki_v1_router
  src/yashigani/backoffice/app.py             — include_router; 256-byte limit
  src/yashigani/backoffice/templates/dashboard.html — PKI nav + page-pki section
  CHANGELOG.md                                — Added (v2.23.3) PKI entry
  docs/release-notes/v2.23.3.md              — PKI highlights + API changes table

## Enumerate-before-edit grep (SOP 0)

YASHIGANI_PKI_CA_MODE, YASHIGANI_BYO_* env vars are new — no pre-existing
consumers in src/, helm/, docker/, config/. grep output: zero hits.

pki_v1_router consumers:
  src/yashigani/backoffice/routes/__init__.py:47  (import)
  src/yashigani/backoffice/routes/__init__.py:81  (__all__)
  src/yashigani/backoffice/app.py:91              (import from routes)
  src/yashigani/backoffice/app.py:710             (include_router)
  — all four updated in this commit.

No helm values added. No Dockerfile changes. No new runtime dependencies.

Co-authored-by: Maxine <maxine@agnosticsec.com>

Description: Detected a Generic API Key, potentially exposing access to various services and sensitive operations.
Author: Agnostic Security
Message:
feat(auth): HIBP password-breach check on set/change (v2.23.3) (#59)

* feat(auth): HIBP password-breach check on set/change (v2.23.3)

OWASP ASVS V2.1.7: every user-chosen password change is now checked
against the Have I Been Pwned database via k-Anonymity range API.
Breached passwords are rejected with HTTP 422 + clear user message.
System-generated temp passwords are explicitly exempt (check_breach=False).

Changes:
- src/yashigani/auth/password.py
  - hibp_check_enabled(): reads YASHIGANI_HIBP_CHECK_ENABLED (default true)
  - hibp_api_url(): reads YASHIGANI_HIBP_API_URL (default pwnedpasswords.com)
  - check_hibp(): API URL read at call time (env override support); failure
    logging upgraded from DEBUG to WARNING; metric incremented on failure
  - _check_hibp_urllib(): takes api_url param (no module-level constant)
  - PasswordBreachedError: message updated to match ASVS/brief spec:
    "This password has appeared in known data breaches; choose another."
  - hash_password(): gates on hibp_check_enabled() before calling HIBP
  - validate_password_not_breached(): respects opt-out env var
- src/yashigani/auth/__init__.py — export PasswordBreachedError,
  PasswordContextError, check_hibp, validate_password_not_breached,
  hibp_check_enabled, hibp_api_url
- src/yashigani/auth/local_auth.py — check_breach=False on all
  system-generated passwords (create_admin, create_user, full_reset_user)
- src/yashigani/auth/pg_auth.py — same, 3 sites
- src/yashigani/backoffice/routes/auth.py
  - change_password: PasswordBreachedError caught before (ValueError, TypeError)
    → HTTP 422 password_breached (distinct from 400 password_rejected)
  - self_service_password_reset: temp password uses check_breach=False
- src/yashigani/metrics/registry.py — hibp_check_api_unavailable_total counter
- src/tests/unit/test_hibp.py — 46 unit tests (all GREEN)
- src/tests/integration/test_hibp_password_change.py — 6 integration tests (all GREEN)
- docs/yashigani_install_config.md § 20.3 — env-var table + network requirement
- docs/release-notes/v2.23.3.md — new release notes file

Enumerate-before-edit — hash_password call sites with no check_breach=False
(all system-generated; updated):
  grep 'hash_password(' src/yashigani/auth/local_auth.py src/yashigani/auth/pg_auth.py
  local_auth.py:94  create_admin        → check_breach=False
  local_auth.py:114 create_user         → check_breach=False
  local_auth.py:318 full_reset_user     → check_breach=False
  pg_auth.py:86     create_admin        → check_breach=False
  pg_auth.py:105    create_user         → check_breach=False
  pg_auth.py:278    full_reset_user     → check_breach=False
  local_auth.py:286 change_password     → check_breach=True (user-chosen, correct)
  pg_auth.py:247    change_password     → check_breach=True (user-chosen, correct)

Failure mode: fail-open on API unreachability — password change proceeds,
WARNING logged, metric incremented. Documented in code comments + tests + docs.

* feat(auth): admin-panel HIBP API-key configuration (v2.23.3)

Extends PR #59 (HIBP breach check) with operator-supplied API key
management via the admin panel. Full key never returned, logged, or
stored in plaintext — pgcrypto column-level encryption throughout.

## New files
- src/yashigani/db/migrations/versions/0007_auth_settings.py
  Alembic migration: auth_settings table (key TEXT PK,
  value_encrypted BYTEA, updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ, updated_by TEXT).
  No inline pgp_sym_encrypt in DDL (app.aes_key not set at migrate time).
  GRANT to yashigani_app.
- src/yashigani/auth/settings_store.py
  AuthSettingsStore(pool): get_setting / set_setting / get_metadata.
  Uses tenant_transaction() pattern (SET app.aes_key per txn).
- src/yashigani/auth/hibp_config.py
  mask_hibp_key, validate_hibp_key_format, resolve_hibp_api_key
  (priority: DB > env YASHIGANI_HIBP_API_KEY > None), get_hibp_key_status.
- src/yashigani/backoffice/routes/hibp.py
  GET /api/v1/admin/auth/hibp/status (AdminSession)
  PUT /api/v1/admin/auth/hibp/key   (StepUpAdminSession, format-validated)
  DELETE /api/v1/admin/auth/hibp/key (StepUpAdminSession)
  503 guard when store not initialised.
- src/yashigani/backoffice/static/js/hibp.js
  CSP-compliant external JS. loadHibpStatus / saveHibpKey / clearHibpKey
  / testHibpConnection. DOMContentLoaded wiring only. window.loadHibpStatus
  exported for dashboard.js.
- src/tests/unit/test_hibp_admin_key.py        (31 tests, all pass)
- src/tests/integration/test_hibp_admin_key_routes.py (14 tests, all pass)

* fix(auth/hibp): Iris W4/W5/W6 — audit best-effort, SSRF note, Helm secret

Addresses Iris integration-audit warnings on PR #59 (HIBP).

W3 — auth_settings_store init log (triage: no-op).
  The INFO log "auth_settings_store initialised (pgcrypto-backed encrypted
  store)" at app.py:235 is already present and fires on successful init.
  The RuntimeError on missing YASHIGANI_DB_AES_KEY (app.py:224-232) prevents
  silent degradation. The 503 route handler covers request-time failure.
  Iris's concern is met by existing code. No code change required.

W4 — audit write failure propagates as HTTP 500.
  Fixed: wrapped audit_writer.write() in try/except in both PUT /key and
  DELETE /key routes (hibp.py). If audit_writer.write() raises, we log at
  ERROR and continue — the key was already committed to the DB.
  Pattern consistent with _write_audit() in webauthn.py.
  Added W4 regression tests (TestAuditBestEffort: 2 cases).

W5 — httpx vs urllib split / PR #81 SSRF gate dependency.
  Added code comment in password.py check_hibp() marking the PR #81 migration
  point: when PR #81 lands, httpx.get → HttpClient.get, and unit test mock
  targets change. The comment is explicit so the PR #81 author knows what to
  update. No functional change in this PR.

W6 — YASHIGANI_DB_AES_KEY not in Helm values.yaml / backoffice.yaml.
  Fixed: added explicit secretKeyRef for YASHIGANI_DB_AES_KEY in
  helm/yashigani/templates/backoffice.yaml sourcing from
  yashigani-backoffice-secrets.db_aes_key (same secret install.sh writes).
  Added documentation comment in values.yaml explaining the required key,
  format, and generation command.
  Fail-closed: missing K8s Secret key → pod CrashLoopBackOff immediately
  (Kubernetes secretKeyRef is required, not optional).

Message:
feat(auth): HIBP password-breach check on set/change (v2.23.3) (#59)

* feat(auth): HIBP password-breach check on set/change (v2.23.3)

OWASP ASVS V2.1.7: every user-chosen password change is now checked
against the Have I Been Pwned database via k-Anonymity range API.
Breached passwords are rejected with HTTP 422 + clear user message.
System-generated temp passwords are explicitly exempt (check_breach=False).

Changes:
- src/yashigani/auth/password.py
  - hibp_check_enabled(): reads YASHIGANI_HIBP_CHECK_ENABLED (default true)
  - hibp_api_url(): reads YASHIGANI_HIBP_API_URL (default pwnedpasswords.com)
  - check_hibp(): API URL read at call time (env override support); failure
    logging upgraded from DEBUG to WARNING; metric incremented on failure
  - _check_hibp_urllib(): takes api_url param (no module-level constant)
  - PasswordBreachedError: message updated to match ASVS/brief spec:
    "This password has appeared in known data breaches; choose another."
  - hash_password(): gates on hibp_check_enabled() before calling HIBP
  - validate_password_not_breached(): respects opt-out env var
- src/yashigani/auth/__init__.py — export PasswordBreachedError,
  PasswordContextError, check_hibp, validate_password_not_breached,
  hibp_check_enabled, hibp_api_url
- src/yashigani/auth/local_auth.py — check_breach=False on all
  system-generated passwords (create_admin, create_user, full_reset_user)
- src/yashigani/auth/pg_auth.py — same, 3 sites
- src/yashigani/backoffice/routes/auth.py
  - change_password: PasswordBreachedError caught before (ValueError, TypeError)
    → HTTP 422 password_breached (distinct from 400 password_rejected)
  - self_service_password_reset: temp password uses check_breach=False
- src/yashigani/metrics/registry.py — hibp_check_api_unavailable_total counter
- src/tests/unit/test_hibp.py — 46 unit tests (all GREEN)
- src/tests/integration/test_hibp_password_change.py — 6 integration tests (all GREEN)
- docs/yashigani_install_config.md § 20.3 — env-var table + network requirement
- docs/release-notes/v2.23.3.md — new release notes file

WARNING logged, metric incremented. Documented in code comments + tests + docs.

* fix(auth/hibp): Iris W4/W5/W6 — audit best-effort, SSRF note, Helm secret

Addresses Iris integration-audit warnings on PR #59 (HIBP).

W3 — auth_settings_store init log (triage: no-op).
  The INFO log "auth_settings_store initialised (pgcrypto-backed encrypted
  store)" at app.py:235 is already present and fires on successful init.
  The RuntimeError on missing YASHIGANI_DB_AES_KEY (app.py:224-232) prevents
  silent degradation. The 503 route handler covers request-time failure.
  Iris's concern is met by existing code. No code change required.

W4 — audit write failure propagates as HTTP 500.
  Fixed: wrapped audit_writer.write() in try/except in both PUT /key and
  DELETE /key routes (hibp.py). If audit_writer.write() raises, we log at
  ERROR and continue — the key was already committed to the DB.

W5 — httpx vs urllib split / PR #81 SSRF gate dependency.
  Added code comment in password.py check_hibp() marking the PR #81 migration
  point: when PR #81 lands, httpx.get → HttpClient.get, and unit test mock
  targets change. The comment is explicit so the PR #81 author knows what to
  update. No functional change in this PR.

W6 — YASHIGANI_DB_AES_KEY not in Helm values.yaml / backoffice.yaml.
  Fixed: added explicit secretKeyRef for YASHIGANI_DB_AES_KEY in
  helm/yashigani/templates/backoffice.yaml sourcing from
  yashigani-backoffice-secrets.db_aes_key (same secret install.sh writes).

  Fail-closed: missing K8s Secret key → pod CrashLoopBackOff immediately
  (Kubernetes secretKeyRef is required, not optional).

Message:
feat(auth): HIBP password-breach check on set/change (v2.23.3) (#59)

* feat(auth): HIBP password-breach check on set/change (v2.23.3)

OWASP ASVS V2.1.7: every user-chosen password change is now checked
against the Have I Been Pwned database via k-Anonymity range API.
Breached passwords are rejected with HTTP 422 + clear user message.
System-generated temp passwords are explicitly exempt (check_breach=False).

Changes:
- src/yashigani/auth/password.py
  - hibp_check_enabled(): reads YASHIGANI_HIBP_CHECK_ENABLED (default true)
  - hibp_api_url(): reads YASHIGANI_HIBP_API_URL (default pwnedpasswords.com)
  - check_hibp(): API URL read at call time (env override support); failure
    logging upgraded from DEBUG to WARNING; metric incremented on failure
  - _check_hibp_urllib(): takes api_url param (no module-level constant)
  - PasswordBreachedError: message updated to match ASVS/brief spec:
    "This password has appeared in known data breaches; choose another."
  - hash_password(): gates on hibp_check_enabled() before calling HIBP
  - validate_password_not_breached(): respects opt-out env var
- src/yashigani/auth/__init__.py — export PasswordBreachedError,
  PasswordContextError, check_hibp, validate_password_not_breached,
  hibp_check_enabled, hibp_api_url
- src/yashigani/auth/local_auth.py — check_breach=False on all
  system-generated passwords (create_admin, create_user, full_reset_user)
- src/yashigani/auth/pg_auth.py — same, 3 sites
- src/yashigani/backoffice/routes/auth.py
  - change_password: PasswordBreachedError caught before (ValueError, TypeError)
    → HTTP 422 password_breached (distinct from 400 password_rejected)
  - self_service_password_reset: temp password uses check_breach=False
- src/yashigani/metrics/registry.py — hibp_check_api_unavailable_total counter
- src/tests/unit/test_hibp.py — 46 unit tests (all GREEN)
- src/tests/integration/test_hibp_password_change.py — 6 integration tests (all GREEN)
- docs/yashigani_install_config.md § 20.3 — env-var table + network requirement
- docs/release-notes/v2.23.3.md — new release notes file

  local_auth.py:94  create_admin        → check_breach=False
  local_auth.py:114 create_user         → check_breach=False
  local_auth.py:318 full_reset_user     → check_breach=False
  pg_auth.py:86     create_admin        → check_breach=False

WARNING logged, metric incremented. Documented in code comments + tests + docs.

* feat(auth): admin-panel HIBP API-key configuration (v2.23.3)

Extends PR #59 (HIBP breach check) with operator-supplied API key
management via the admin panel. Full key never returned, logged, or
stored in plaintext — pgcrypto column-level encryption throughout.

  Alembic migration: auth_settings table (key TEXT PK,
  value_encrypted BYTEA, updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ, updated_by TEXT).
  No inline pgp_sym_encrypt in DDL (app.aes_key not set at migrate time).

  AuthSettingsStore(pool): get_setting / set_setting / get_metadata.

* fix(auth/hibp): Iris W4/W5/W6 — audit best-effort, SSRF note, Helm secret

Addresses Iris integration-audit warnings on PR #59 (HIBP).

W3 — auth_settings_store init log (triage: no-op).
  The INFO log "auth_settings_store initialised (pgcrypto-backed encrypted
  store)" at app.py:235 is already present and fires on successful init.
  The RuntimeError on missing YASHIGANI_DB_AES_KEY (app.py:224-232) prevents
  silent degradation. The 503 route handler covers request-time failure.
  Iris's concern is met by existing code. No code change required.

W4 — audit write failure propagates as HTTP 500.
  Fixed: wrapped audit_writer.write() in try/except in both PUT /key and
  DELETE /key routes (hibp.py). If audit_writer.write() raises, we log at
  ERROR and continue — the key was already committed to the DB.
  Pattern consistent with _write_audit() in webauthn.py.
  Added W4 regression tests (TestAuditBestEffort: 2 cases).

W5 — httpx vs urllib split / PR #81 SSRF gate dependency.
  Added code comment in password.py check_hibp() marking the PR #81 migration
  point: when PR #81 lands, httpx.get → HttpClient.get, and unit test mock
  targets change. The comment is explicit so the PR #81 author knows what to
  update. No functional change in this PR.

W6 — YASHIGANI_DB_AES_KEY not in Helm values.yaml / backoffice.yaml.
  Fixed: added explicit secretKeyRef for YASHIGANI_DB_AES_KEY in
  helm/yashigani/templates/backoffice.yaml sourcing from
  yashigani-backoffice-secrets.db_aes_key (same secret install.sh writes).
  Added documentation comment in values.yaml explaining the required key,
  format, and generation command.
  Fail-closed: missing K8s Secret key → pod CrashLoopBackOff immediately
  (Kubernetes secretKeyRef is required, not optional).

Message:
feat(auth): HIBP password-breach check on set/change (v2.23.3) (#59)

* feat(auth): HIBP password-breach check on set/change (v2.23.3)

OWASP ASVS V2.1.7: every user-chosen password change is now checked
against the Have I Been Pwned database via k-Anonymity range API.
Breached passwords are rejected with HTTP 422 + clear user message.
System-generated temp passwords are explicitly exempt (check_breach=False).

Changes:
- src/yashigani/auth/password.py
  - hibp_check_enabled(): reads YASHIGANI_HIBP_CHECK_ENABLED (default true)
  - hibp_api_url(): reads YASHIGANI_HIBP_API_URL (default pwnedpasswords.com)
  - check_hibp(): API URL read at call time (env override support); failure
    logging upgraded from DEBUG to WARNING; metric incremented on failure
  - _check_hibp_urllib(): takes api_url param (no module-level constant)
  - PasswordBreachedError: message updated to match ASVS/brief spec:
    "This password has appeared in known data breaches; choose another."
  - hash_password(): gates on hibp_check_enabled() before calling HIBP
  - validate_password_not_breached(): respects opt-out env var
- src/yashigani/auth/__init__.py — export PasswordBreachedError,
  PasswordContextError, check_hibp, validate_password_not_breached,
  hibp_check_enabled, hibp_api_url
- src/yashigani/auth/local_auth.py — check_breach=False on all
  system-generated passwords (create_admin, create_user, full_reset_user)
- src/yashigani/auth/pg_auth.py — same, 3 sites
- src/yashigani/backoffice/routes/auth.py
  - change_password: PasswordBreachedError caught before (ValueError, TypeError)
    → HTTP 422 password_breached (distinct from 400 password_rejected)
  - self_service_password_reset: temp password uses check_breach=False
- src/yashigani/metrics/registry.py — hibp_check_api_unavailable_total counter
- src/tests/unit/test_hibp.py — 46 unit tests (all GREEN)
- src/tests/integration/test_hibp_password_change.py — 6 integration tests (all GREEN)
- docs/yashigani_install_config.md § 20.3 — env-var table + network requirement
- docs/release-notes/v2.23.3.md — new release notes file

Enumerate-before-edit — hash_password call sites with no check_breach=False
(all system-generated; updated):
  grep 'hash_password(' src/yashigani/auth/local_auth.py src/yashigani/auth/pg_auth.py
  local_auth.py:94  create_admin        → check_breach=False
  local_auth.py:114 create_user         → check_breach=False
  local_auth.py:318 full_reset_user     → check_breach=False
  pg_auth.py:86     create_admin        → check_breach=False
  pg_auth.py:105    create_user         → check_breach=False
  pg_auth.py:278    full_reset_user     → check_breach=False
  local_auth.py:286 change_password     → check_breach=True (user-chosen, correct)
  pg_auth.py:247    change_password     → check_breach=True (user-chosen, correct)

Failure mode: fail-open on API unreachability — password change proceeds,
WARNING logged, metric incremented. Documented in code comments + tests + docs.

* feat(auth): admin-panel HIBP API-key configuration (v2.23.3)

Extends PR #59 (HIBP breach check) with operator-supplied API key
management via the admin panel. Full key never returned, logged, or
stored in plaintext — pgcrypto column-level encryption throughout.

## New files
- src/yashigani/db/migrations/versions/0007_auth_settings.py
  Alembic migration: auth_settings table (key TEXT PK,
  value_encrypted BYTEA, updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ, updated_by TEXT).
  No inline pgp_sym_encrypt in DDL (app.aes_key not set at migrate time).
  GRANT to yashigani_app.
- src/yashigani/auth/settings_store.py
  AuthSettingsStore(pool): get_setting / set_setting / get_metadata.
  Uses tenant_transaction() pattern (SET app.aes_key per txn).
- src/yashigani/auth/hibp_config.py
  mask_hibp_key, validate_hibp_key_format, resolve_hibp_api_key
  (priority: DB > env YASHIGANI_HIBP_API_KEY > None), get_hibp_key_status.
- src/yashigani/backoffice/routes/hibp.py
  GET /api/v1/admin/auth/hibp/status (AdminSession)
  PUT /api/v1/admin/auth/hibp/key   (StepUpAdminSession, format-validated)
  DELETE /api/v1/admin/auth/hibp/key (StepUpAdminSession)
  503 guard when store not initialised.
- src/yashigani/backoffice/static/js/hibp.js
  CSP-compliant external JS. loadHibpStatus / saveHibpKey / clearHibpKey
  / testHibpConnection. DOMContentLoaded wiring only. window.loadHibpStatus
  exported for dashboard.js.
- src/tests/unit/test_hibp_admin_key.py        (31 tests, all pass)
- src/tests/integration/test_hibp_admin_key_routes.py (14 tests, all pass)
- src/tests/playwright/test_hibp_admin_ui.py    (PW-HIBP-01..07)

## Modified files
- src/yashigani/auth/password.py
  check_hibp(password, *, api_key=None) — if key set adds hibp-api-key

  _check_hibp_urllib same for stdlib path.

  EventType.HIBP_API_KEY_UPDATED / HIBP_API_KEY_CLEARED.

  Barrel-exports: AuthSettingsStore, mask_hibp_key,

- src/yashigani/backoffice/routes/__init__.py
  hibp_router import + __all__ entry.

  Body-limit entry (/api/v1/admin/auth/hibp → 512 bytes).
  Lifespan: AuthSettingsStore wired from pool.
  include_router(hibp_router, prefix=...).
- src/yashigani/backoffice/state.py
  auth_settings_store: Optional[Any] = None field added.
- src/yashigani/backoffice/static/js/dashboard.js
  loadSettings() calls window.loadHibpStatus() if defined.
- src/yashigani/backoffice/templates/dashboard.html
  HIBP panel in #page-settings. hibp.js <script defer>.
- docs/yashigani_install_config.md
  §20.3: YASHIGANI_HIBP_API_KEY env var + admin-panel key subsection.
- docs/release-notes/v2.23.3.md
  Admin-panel HIBP API key highlights + API endpoint table.

grep -rEn "hibp_api_key|hibp_check_enabled|check_hibp|hibp_api_url" src/

  store)" at app.py:235 is already present and fires on successful init.
  The RuntimeError on missing YASHIGANI_DB_AES_KEY (app.py:224-232) prevents
  silent degradation. The 503 route handler covers request-time failure.
  Iris's concern is met by existing code. No code change required.

W4 — audit write failure propagates as HTTP 500.
  Fixed: wrapped audit_writer.write() in try/except in both PUT /key and
  DELETE /key routes (hibp.py). If audit_writer.write() raises, we log at
  ERROR and continue — the key was already committed to the DB.
  Pattern consistent with _write_audit() in webauthn.py.
  Added W4 regression tests (TestAuditBestEffort: 2 cases).

W5 — httpx vs urllib split / PR #81 SSRF gate dependency.
  Added code comment in password.py check_hibp() marking the PR #81 migration
  point: when PR #81 lands, httpx.get → HttpClient.get, and unit test mock
  targets change. The comment is explicit so the PR #81 author knows what to
  update. No functional change in this PR.

W6 — YASHIGANI_DB_AES_KEY not in Helm values.yaml / backoffice.yaml.
  Fixed: added explicit secretKeyRef for YASHIGANI_DB_AES_KEY in
  helm/yashigani/templates/backoffice.yaml sourcing from
  yashigani-backoffice-secrets.db_aes_key (same secret install.sh writes).
  Added documentation comment in values.yaml explaining the required key,
  format, and generation command.
  Fail-closed: missing K8s Secret key → pod CrashLoopBackOff immediately
  (Kubernetes secretKeyRef is required, not optional).

Message:
feat(auth): HIBP password-breach check on set/change (v2.23.3) (#59)

* feat(auth): HIBP password-breach check on set/change (v2.23.3)

OWASP ASVS V2.1.7: every user-chosen password change is now checked
against the Have I Been Pwned database via k-Anonymity range API.
Breached passwords are rejected with HTTP 422 + clear user message.
System-generated temp passwords are explicitly exempt (check_breach=False).

Changes:
- src/yashigani/auth/password.py
  - hibp_check_enabled(): reads YASHIGANI_HIBP_CHECK_ENABLED (default true)
  - hibp_api_url(): reads YASHIGANI_HIBP_API_URL (default pwnedpasswords.com)
  - check_hibp(): API URL read at call time (env override support); failure
    logging upgraded from DEBUG to WARNING; metric incremented on failure
  - _check_hibp_urllib(): takes api_url param (no module-level constant)
  - PasswordBreachedError: message updated to match ASVS/brief spec:
    "This password has appeared in known data breaches; choose another."
  - hash_password(): gates on hibp_check_enabled() before calling HIBP
  - validate_password_not_breached(): respects opt-out env var
- src/yashigani/auth/__init__.py — export PasswordBreachedError,
  PasswordContextError, check_hibp, validate_password_not_breached,
  hibp_check_enabled, hibp_api_url
- src/yashigani/auth/local_auth.py — check_breach=False on all
  system-generated passwords (create_admin, create_user, full_reset_user)
- src/yashigani/auth/pg_auth.py — same, 3 sites
- src/yashigani/backoffice/routes/auth.py
  - change_password: PasswordBreachedError caught before (ValueError, TypeError)
    → HTTP 422 password_breached (distinct from 400 password_rejected)
  - self_service_password_reset: temp password uses check_breach=False
- src/yashigani/metrics/registry.py — hibp_check_api_unavailable_total counter
- src/tests/unit/test_hibp.py — 46 unit tests (all GREEN)
- src/tests/integration/test_hibp_password_change.py — 6 integration tests (all GREEN)
- docs/yashigani_install_config.md § 20.3 — env-var table + network requirement
- docs/release-notes/v2.23.3.md — new release notes file

Enumerate-before-edit — hash_password call sites with no check_breach=False
(all system-generated; updated):
  grep 'hash_password(' src/yashigani/auth/local_auth.py src/yashigani/auth/pg_auth.py
  local_auth.py:94  create_admin        → check_breach=False
  local_auth.py:114 create_user         → check_breach=False
  local_auth.py:318 full_reset_user     → check_breach=False
  pg_auth.py:86     create_admin        → check_breach=False
  pg_auth.py:105    create_user         → check_breach=False
  pg_auth.py:278    full_reset_user     → check_breach=False
  local_auth.py:286 change_password     → check_breach=True (user-chosen, correct)
  pg_auth.py:247    change_password     → check_breach=True (user-chosen, correct)

Failure mode: fail-open on API unreachability — password change proceeds,
WARNING logged, metric incremented. Documented in code comments + tests + docs.

* feat(auth): admin-panel HIBP API-key configuration (v2.23.3)

Extends PR #59 (HIBP breach check) with operator-supplied API key
management via the admin panel. Full key never returned, logged, or
stored in plaintext — pgcrypto column-level encryption throughout.

## New files
- src/yashigani/db/migrations/versions/0007_auth_settings.py
  Alembic migration: auth_settings table (key TEXT PK,
  value_encrypted BYTEA, updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ, updated_by TEXT).
  No inline pgp_sym_encrypt in DDL (app.aes_key not set at migrate time).
  GRANT to yashigani_app.
- src/yashigani/auth/settings_store.py
  AuthSettingsStore(pool): get_setting / set_setting / get_metadata.
  Uses tenant_transaction() pattern (SET app.aes_key per txn).
- src/yashigani/auth/hibp_config.py
  mask_hibp_key, validate_hibp_key_format, resolve_hibp_api_key
  (priority: DB > env YASHIGANI_HIBP_API_KEY > None), get_hibp_key_status.
- src/yashigani/backoffice/routes/hibp.py
  GET /api/v1/admin/auth/hibp/status (AdminSession)
  PUT /api/v1/admin/auth/hibp/key   (StepUpAdminSession, format-validated)
  DELETE /api/v1/admin/auth/hibp/key (StepUpAdminSession)
  503 guard when store not initialised.
- src/yashigani/backoffice/static/js/hibp.js
  CSP-compliant external JS. loadHibpStatus / saveHibpKey / clearHibpKey
  / testHibpConnection. DOMContentLoaded wiring only. window.loadHibpStatus
  exported for dashboard.js.
- src/tests/unit/test_hibp_admin_key.py        (31 tests, all pass)
- src/tests/integration/test_hibp_admin_key_routes.py (14 tests, all pass)
- src/tests/playwright/test_hibp_admin_ui.py    (PW-HIBP-01..07)

## Modified files
- src/yashigani/auth/password.py
  check_hibp(password, *, api_key=None) — if key set adds hibp-api-key
  header; key never logged.
  _check_hibp_urllib same for stdlib path.
- src/yashigani/audit/schema.py
  EventType.HIBP_API_KEY_UPDATED / HIBP_API_KEY_CLEARED.
  HibpApiKeyUpdatedEvent (masked_key_hint only, masking_applied=True).
  HibpApiKeyClearedEvent (masking_applied=True).
- src/yashigani/auth/__init__.py
  Barrel-exports: AuthSettingsStore, mask_hibp_key,
  validate_hibp_key_format, resolve_hibp_api_key, get_hibp_key_status.
- src/yashigani/backoffice/routes/__init__.py
  hibp_router import + __all__ entry.
- src/yashigani/backoffice/app.py
  Body-limit entry (/api/v1/admin/auth/hibp → 512 bytes).
  Lifespan: AuthSettingsStore wired from pool.
  include_router(hibp_router, prefix=...).
- src/yashigani/backoffice/state.py
  auth_settings_store: Optional[Any] = None field added.
- src/yashigani/backoffice/static/js/dashboard.js
  loadSettings() calls window.loadHibpStatus() if defined.
- src/yashigani/backoffice/templates/dashboard.html
  HIBP panel in #page-settings. hibp.js <script defer>.
- docs/yashigani_install_config.md
  §20.3: YASHIGANI_HIBP_API_KEY env var + admin-panel key subsection.
- docs/release-notes/v2.23.3.md
  Admin-panel HIBP API key highlights + API endpoint table.

## SOP 0 — consumer grep
grep -rEn "hibp_api_key|hibp_check_enabled|check_hibp|hibp_api_url" src/
All new consumers are in the files listed above. No orphaned references.

## Security invariants
- Full key never in any API response (masked hint only)
- Full key never logged at any level
- Audit events: masking_applied=True immutable floor
- PUT + DELETE require StepUpAdminSession (fresh TOTP)
- DB value encrypted with pgp_sym_encrypt / app.aes_key
- Resolution priority: DB-panel > YASHIGANI_HIBP_API_KEY env > anonymous

Message:
feat(auth): HIBP password-breach check on set/change (v2.23.3) (#59)

* feat(auth): HIBP password-breach check on set/change (v2.23.3)

OWASP ASVS V2.1.7: every user-chosen password change is now checked
against the Have I Been Pwned database via k-Anonymity range API.
Breached passwords are rejected with HTTP 422 + clear user message.
System-generated temp passwords are explicitly exempt (check_breach=False).

Changes:
- src/yashigani/auth/password.py
  - hibp_check_enabled(): reads YASHIGANI_HIBP_CHECK_ENABLED (default true)
  - hibp_api_url(): reads YASHIGANI_HIBP_API_URL (default pwnedpasswords.com)
  - check_hibp(): API URL read at call time (env override support); failure
    logging upgraded from DEBUG to WARNING; metric incremented on failure
  - _check_hibp_urllib(): takes api_url param (no module-level constant)
  - PasswordBreachedError: message updated to match ASVS/brief spec:
    "This password has appeared in known data breaches; choose another."
  - hash_password(): gates on hibp_check_enabled() before calling HIBP
  - validate_password_not_breached(): respects opt-out env var
- src/yashigani/auth/__init__.py — export PasswordBreachedError,
  PasswordContextError, check_hibp, validate_password_not_breached,
  hibp_check_enabled, hibp_api_url
- src/yashigani/auth/local_auth.py — check_breach=False on all
  system-generated passwords (create_admin, create_user, full_reset_user)
- src/yashigani/auth/pg_auth.py — same, 3 sites
- src/yashigani/backoffice/routes/auth.py
  - change_password: PasswordBreachedError caught before (ValueError, TypeError)
    → HTTP 422 password_breached (distinct from 400 password_rejected)
  - self_service_password_reset: temp password uses check_breach=False
- src/yashigani/metrics/registry.py — hibp_check_api_unavailable_total counter
- src/tests/unit/test_hibp.py — 46 unit tests (all GREEN)
- src/tests/integration/test_hibp_password_change.py — 6 integration tests (all GREEN)
- docs/yashigani_install_config.md § 20.3 — env-var table + network requirement
- docs/release-notes/v2.23.3.md — new release notes file

Extends PR #59 (HIBP breach check) with operator-supplied API key
management via the admin panel. Full key never returned, logged, or
stored in plaintext — pgcrypto column-level encryption throughout.

  Alembic migration: auth_settings table (key TEXT PK,

  / testHibpConnection. DOMContentLoaded wiring only. window.loadHibpStatus
  exported for dashboard.js.
- src/tests/unit/test_hibp_admin_key.py        (31 tests, all pass)
- src/tests/integration/test_hibp_admin_key_routes.py (14 tests, all pass)
- src/tests/playwright/test_hibp_admin_ui.py    (PW-HIBP-01..07)

  check_hibp(password, *, api_key=None) — if key set adds hibp-api-key

  hibp_router import + __all__ entry.

* fix(auth/hibp): Iris W4/W5/W6 — audit best-effort, SSRF note, Helm secret

Addresses Iris integration-audit warnings on PR #59 (HIBP).

W3 — auth_settings_store init log (triage: no-op).
  The INFO log "auth_settings_store initialised (pgcrypto-backed encrypted
  store)" at app.py:235 is already present and fires on successful init.
  The RuntimeError on missing YASHIGANI_DB_AES_KEY (app.py:224-232) prevents
  silent degradation. The 503 route handler covers request-time failure.
  Iris's concern is met by existing code. No code change required.

W4 — audit write failure propagates as HTTP 500.
  Fixed: wrapped audit_writer.write() in try/except in both PUT /key and
  DELETE /key routes (hibp.py). If audit_writer.write() raises, we log at
  ERROR and continue — the key was already committed to the DB.
  Pattern consistent with _write_audit() in webauthn.py.
  Added W4 regression tests (TestAuditBestEffort: 2 cases).

W5 — httpx vs urllib split / PR #81 SSRF gate dependency.
  Added code comment in password.py check_hibp() marking the PR #81 migration
  point: when PR #81 lands, httpx.get → HttpClient.get, and unit test mock
  targets change. The comment is explicit so the PR #81 author knows what to
  update. No functional change in this PR.

W6 — YASHIGANI_DB_AES_KEY not in Helm values.yaml / backoffice.yaml.
  Fixed: added explicit secretKeyRef for YASHIGANI_DB_AES_KEY in
  helm/yashigani/templates/backoffice.yaml sourcing from
  yashigani-backoffice-secrets.db_aes_key (same secret install.sh writes).
  Added documentation comment in values.yaml explaining the required key,
  format, and generation command.
  Fail-closed: missing K8s Secret key → pod CrashLoopBackOff immediately
  (Kubernetes secretKeyRef is required, not optional).

Message:
feat(auth): HIBP password-breach check on set/change (v2.23.3) (#59)

* feat(auth): HIBP password-breach check on set/change (v2.23.3)

OWASP ASVS V2.1.7: every user-chosen password change is now checked
against the Have I Been Pwned database via k-Anonymity range API.
Breached passwords are rejected with HTTP 422 + clear user message.
System-generated temp passwords are explicitly exempt (check_breach=False).

Changes:
- src/yashigani/auth/password.py
  - hibp_check_enabled(): reads YASHIGANI_HIBP_CHECK_ENABLED (default true)
  - hibp_api_url(): reads YASHIGANI_HIBP_API_URL (default pwnedpasswords.com)
  - check_hibp(): API URL read at call time (env override support); failure
    logging upgraded from DEBUG to WARNING; metric incremented on failure
  - _check_hibp_urllib(): takes api_url param (no module-level constant)
  - PasswordBreachedError: message updated to match ASVS/brief spec:
    "This password has appeared in known data breaches; choose another."
  - hash_password(): gates on hibp_check_enabled() before calling HIBP
  - validate_password_not_breached(): respects opt-out env var
- src/yashigani/auth/__init__.py — export PasswordBreachedError,
  PasswordContextError, check_hibp, validate_password_not_breached,
  hibp_check_enabled, hibp_api_url
- src/yashigani/auth/local_auth.py — check_breach=False on all
  system-generated passwords (create_admin, create_user, full_reset_user)
- src/yashigani/auth/pg_auth.py — same, 3 sites
- src/yashigani/backoffice/routes/auth.py
  - change_password: PasswordBreachedError caught before (ValueError, TypeError)
    → HTTP 422 password_breached (distinct from 400 password_rejected)
  - self_service_password_reset: temp password uses check_breach=False
- src/yashigani/metrics/registry.py — hibp_check_api_unavailable_total counter
- src/tests/unit/test_hibp.py — 46 unit tests (all GREEN)
- src/tests/integration/test_hibp_password_change.py — 6 integration tests (all GREEN)
- docs/yashigani_install_config.md § 20.3 — env-var table + network requirement
- docs/release-notes/v2.23.3.md — new release notes file

Enumerate-before-edit — hash_password call sites with no check_breach=False
(all system-generated; updated):
  grep 'hash_password(' src/yashigani/auth/local_auth.py src/yashigani/auth/pg_auth.py
  local_auth.py:94  create_admin        → check_breach=False
  local_auth.py:114 create_user         → check_breach=False
  local_auth.py:318 full_reset_user     → check_breach=False
  pg_auth.py:86     create_admin        → check_breach=False
  pg_auth.py:105    create_user         → check_breach=False
  pg_auth.py:278    full_reset_user     → check_breach=False
  local_auth.py:286 change_password     → check_breach=True (user-chosen, correct)
  pg_auth.py:247    change_password     → check_breach=True (user-chosen, correct)

Failure mode: fail-open on API unreachability — password change proceeds,
WARNING logged, metric incremented. Documented in code comments + tests + docs.

* feat(auth): admin-panel HIBP API-key configuration (v2.23.3)

Extends PR #59 (HIBP breach check) with operator-supplied API key
management via the admin panel. Full key never returned, logged, or
stored in plaintext — pgcrypto column-level encryption throughout.

## New files
- src/yashigani/db/migrations/versions/0007_auth_settings.py
  Alembic migration: auth_settings table (key TEXT PK,
  value_encrypted BYTEA, updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ, updated_by TEXT).
  No inline pgp_sym_encrypt in DDL (app.aes_key not set at migrate time).
  GRANT to yashigani_app.
- src/yashigani/auth/settings_store.py
  AuthSettingsStore(pool): get_setting / set_setting / get_metadata.
  Uses tenant_transaction() pattern (SET app.aes_key per txn).
- src/yashigani/auth/hibp_config.py
  mask_hibp_key, validate_hibp_key_format, resolve_hibp_api_key
  (priority: DB > env YASHIGANI_HIBP_API_KEY > None), get_hibp_key_status.
- src/yashigani/backoffice/routes/hibp.py
  GET /api/v1/admin/auth/hibp/status (AdminSession)
  PUT /api/v1/admin/auth/hibp/key   (StepUpAdminSession, format-validated)
  DELETE /api/v1/admin/auth/hibp/key (StepUpAdminSession)
  503 guard when store not initialised.
- src/yashigani/backoffice/static/js/hibp.js
  CSP-compliant external JS. loadHibpStatus / saveHibpKey / clearHibpKey
  / testHibpConnection. DOMContentLoaded wiring only. window.loadHibpStatus
  exported for dashboard.js.
- src/tests/unit/test_hibp_admin_key.py        (31 tests, all pass)
- src/tests/integration/test_hibp_admin_key_routes.py (14 tests, all pass)
- src/tests/playwright/test_hibp_admin_ui.py    (PW-HIBP-01..07)

## Modified files
- src/yashigani/auth/password.py
  check_hibp(password, *, api_key=None) — if key set adds hibp-api-key
  header; key never logged.
  _check_hibp_urllib same for stdlib path.
- src/yashigani/audit/schema.py
  EventType.HIBP_API_KEY_UPDATED / HIBP_API_KEY_CLEARED.
  HibpApiKeyUpdatedEvent (masked_key_hint only, masking_applied=True).
  HibpApiKeyClearedEvent (masking_applied=True).
- src/yashigani/auth/__init__.py
  Barrel-exports: AuthSettingsStore, mask_hibp_key,
  validate_hibp_key_format, resolve_hibp_api_key, get_hibp_key_status.
- src/yashigani/backoffice/routes/__init__.py
  hibp_router import + __all__ entry.
- src/yashigani/backoffice/app.py
  Body-limit entry (/api/v1/admin/auth/hibp → 512 bytes).
  Lifespan: AuthSettingsStore wired from pool.
  include_router(hibp_router, prefix=...).
- src/yashigani/backoffice/state.py
  auth_settings_store: Optional[Any] = None field added.
- src/yashigani/backoffice/static/js/dashboard.js
  loadSettings() calls window.loadHibpStatus() if defined.
- src/yashigani/backoffice/templates/dashboard.html
  HIBP panel in #page-settings. hibp.js <script defer>.
- docs/yashigani_install_config.md
  §20.3: YASHIGANI_HIBP_API_KEY env var + admin-panel key subsection.
- docs/release-notes/v2.23.3.md
  Admin-panel HIBP API key highlights + API endpoint table.

## SOP 0 — consumer grep
grep -rEn "hibp_api_key|hibp_check_enabled|check_hibp|hibp_api_url" src/
All new consumers are in the files listed above. No orphaned references.

## Security invariants
- Full key never in any API response (masked hint only)
- Full key never logged at any level
- Audit events: masking_applied=True immutable floor
- PUT + DELETE require StepUpAdminSession (fresh TOTP)
- DB value encrypted with pgp_sym_encrypt / app.aes_key
- Resolution priority: DB-panel > YASHIGANI_HIBP_API_KEY env > anonymous

## Pre-existing test failure (unrelated)
test_gateway_auth.py::TestAgentRegistry::test_register_returns_id_and_token
fails due to fakeredis not supporting EVAL command. Confirmed pre-existing
on 5d60de9 (before this change). Not introduced by this commit.

* fix(auth): B1+B2 — renumber migration 0007→0008 + AES key fail-fast

B1 (Iris blocker): PR #59's migration 0007_auth_settings collides with
PR #62's 0007_webauthn_admin_creds. Per Iris's recommendation, #62
keeps 0007 (extends existing webauthn_credentials table); #59 becomes
0008 (new auth_settings table). Linear chain: 0006→0007→0008.

  - Renamed: 0007_auth_settings.py → 0008_auth_settings.py
  - Updated: revision = "0008", down_revision = "0007"
  - Updated: docs/release-notes/v2.23.3.md (two migration name refs)

B2 (Iris blocker): YASHIGANI_DB_AES_KEY not documented in env-var table;
no startup fail-fast when unset.

  - Added fail-fast RuntimeError in lifespan before AuthSettingsStore init:
    empty/missing YASHIGANI_DB_AES_KEY → explicit RuntimeError at startup.
    (An empty key causes pgcrypto to encrypt with empty passphrase — silent
    data-security failure. Fail closed per SOP 1.)
  - Added INFO log confirming auth_settings_store init success.
  - Added YASHIGANI_DB_AES_KEY to env-var table in docs/yashigani_install_config.md
    (Required: Yes; generate with openssl rand -hex 32).

Enumerate-before-edit (SOP 0):
  grep "0007_auth_settings" → 2 doc refs + migration file (all updated)
  grep "YASHIGANI_DB_AES_KEY" in app.py → 0 existing refs (new guard added)
  grep "YASHIGANI_DB_AES_KEY" in docs → 1 existing ref in §20.3, added to §4.2 table

Iris finding ref: PR #59 comment 4406366184, Blockers 1 + 2

* fix(auth/hibp): Iris W4/W5/W6 — audit best-effort, SSRF note, Helm secret

Addresses Iris integration-audit warnings on PR #59 (HIBP).

W3 — auth_settings_store init log (triage: no-op).
  The INFO log "auth_settings_store initialised (pgcrypto-backed encrypted
  store)" at app.py:235 is already present and fires on successful init.
  The RuntimeError on missing YASHIGANI_DB_AES_KEY (app.py:224-232) prevents
  silent degradation. The 503 route handler covers request-time failure.
  Iris's concern is met by existing code. No code change required.

W4 — audit write failure propagates as HTTP 500.
  Fixed: wrapped audit_writer.write() in try/except in both PUT /key and
  DELETE /key routes (hibp.py). If audit_writer.write() raises, we log at
  ERROR and continue — the key was already committed to the DB.
  Pattern consistent with _write_audit() in webauthn.py.
  Added W4 regression tests (TestAuditBestEffort: 2 cases).

W5 — httpx vs urllib split / PR #81 SSRF gate dependency.
  Added code comment in password.py check_hibp() marking the PR #81 migration
  point: when PR #81 lands, httpx.get → HttpClient.get, and unit test mock
  targets change. The comment is explicit so the PR #81 author knows what to
  update. No functional change in this PR.

W6 — YASHIGANI_DB_AES_KEY not in Helm values.yaml / backoffice.yaml.
  Fixed: added explicit secretKeyRef for YASHIGANI_DB_AES_KEY in
  helm/yashigani/templates/backoffice.yaml sourcing from
  yashigani-backoffice-secrets.db_aes_key (same secret install.sh writes).
  Added documentation comment in values.yaml explaining the required key,
  format, and generation command.
  Fail-closed: missing K8s Secret key → pod CrashLoopBackOff immediately
  (Kubernetes secretKeyRef is required, not optional).

Enumerate-before-edit: grep for YASHIGANI_DB_AES_KEY:
  install.sh — already present (lines 86, 225, 1344-1421)
  src/yashigani/backoffice/app.py — already present (lines 223-232)
  helm/yashigani/templates/backoffice.yaml — added here
  helm/yashigani/values.yaml — documentation added here
  docs/release-notes/v2.23.3.md — already present
  docs/yashigani_install_config.md — already present

* fix(test): add SSRF policy bypass fixture to HIBP integration test

PR #81 added _hibp_http_client()._check_policy() to check_hibp() before
any outbound request. The integration test test_api_url_override uses an
http:// override URL that is now blocked by the SSRF policy (allow_http=False).

Add the same autouse _bypass_ssrf_policy fixture used in the HIBP unit tests
so the integration test remains network-free and focused on the URL-routing
behaviour rather than SSRF policy enforcement (which is tested separately in
test_v2233_hibp_route_through_httpclient.py).

grep consumers: src/tests/integration/test_hibp_password_change.py:133 (only caller)

  Alembic migration: auth_settings table (key TEXT PK,
  value_encrypted BYTEA, updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ, updated_by TEXT).
  No inline pgp_sym_encrypt in DDL (app.aes_key not set at migrate time).
  GRANT to yashigani_app.
- src/yashigani/auth/settings_store.py
  AuthSettingsStore(pool): get_setting / set_setting / get_metadata.
  Uses tenant_transaction() pattern (SET app.aes_key per txn).
- src/yashigani/auth/hibp_config.py
  mask_hibp_key, validate_hibp_key_format, resolve_hibp_api_key
  (priority: DB > env YASHIGANI_HIBP_API_KEY > None), get_hibp_key_status.
- src/yashigani/backoffice/routes/hibp.py
  GET /api/v1/admin/auth/hibp/status (AdminSession)
  PUT /api/v1/admin/auth/hibp/key   (StepUpAdminSession, format-validated)
  DELETE /api/v1/admin/auth/hibp/key (StepUpAdminSession)
  503 guard when store not initialised.
- src/yashigani/backoffice/static/js/hibp.js
  CSP-compliant external JS. loadHibpStatus / saveHibpKey / clearHibpKey
  / testHibpConnection. DOMContentLoaded wiring only. window.loadHibpStatus
  exported for dashboard.js.
- src/tests/unit/test_hibp_admin_key.py        (31 tests, all pass)
- src/tests/integration/test_hibp_admin_key_routes.py (14 tests, all pass)
- src/tests/playwright/test_hibp_admin_ui.py    (PW-HIBP-01..07)

## Modified files
- src/yashigani/auth/password.py
  check_hibp(password, *, api_key=None) — if key set adds hibp-api-key
  header; key never logged.
  _check_hibp_urllib same for stdlib path.

  HibpApiKeyUpdatedEvent (masked_key_hint only, masking_applied=True).

  Admin-panel HIBP API key highlights + API endpoint table.

## Security invariants
- Full key never in any API response (masked hint only)
- Full key never logged at any level
- Audit events: masking_applied=True immutable floor
- PUT + DELETE require StepUpAdminSession (fresh TOTP)
- DB value encrypted with pgp_sym_encrypt / app.aes_key
- Resolution priority: DB-panel > YASHIGANI_HIBP_API_KEY env > anonymous

- Audit events: masking_applied=True immutable floor
- PUT + DELETE require StepUpAdminSession (fresh TOTP)
- DB value encrypted with pgp_sym_encrypt / app.aes_key
- Resolution priority: DB-panel > YASHIGANI_HIBP_API_KEY env > anonymous

test_gateway_auth.py::TestAgentRegistry::test_register_returns_id_and_token
