on 5d60de9 (before this change). Not introduced by this commit.

Description: Detected a Generic API Key, potentially exposing access to various services and sensitive operations.
Author: Tom
Message:
feat(auth): admin-panel HIBP API-key configuration (v2.23.3)

Extends PR #59 (HIBP breach check) with operator-supplied API key
management via the admin panel. Full key never returned, logged, or
stored in plaintext — pgcrypto column-level encryption throughout.

## New files
- src/yashigani/db/migrations/versions/0007_auth_settings.py
  Alembic migration: auth_settings table (key TEXT PK,
  value_encrypted BYTEA, updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ, updated_by TEXT).
  No inline pgp_sym_encrypt in DDL (app.aes_key not set at migrate time).
  GRANT to yashigani_app.
- src/yashigani/auth/settings_store.py
  AuthSettingsStore(pool): get_setting / set_setting / get_metadata.
  Uses tenant_transaction() pattern (SET app.aes_key per txn).
- src/yashigani/auth/hibp_config.py
  mask_hibp_key, validate_hibp_key_format, resolve_hibp_api_key
  (priority: DB > env YASHIGANI_HIBP_API_KEY > None), get_hibp_key_status.
- src/yashigani/backoffice/routes/hibp.py
  GET /api/v1/admin/auth/hibp/status (AdminSession)
  PUT /api/v1/admin/auth/hibp/key   (StepUpAdminSession, format-validated)
  DELETE /api/v1/admin/auth/hibp/key (StepUpAdminSession)
  503 guard when store not initialised.
- src/yashigani/backoffice/static/js/hibp.js
  CSP-compliant external JS. loadHibpStatus / saveHibpKey / clearHibpKey
  / testHibpConnection. DOMContentLoaded wiring only. window.loadHibpStatus
  exported for dashboard.js.
- src/tests/unit/test_hibp_admin_key.py        (31 tests, all pass)
- src/tests/integration/test_hibp_admin_key_routes.py (14 tests, all pass)
- src/tests/playwright/test_hibp_admin_ui.py    (PW-HIBP-01..07)

## Modified files
- src/yashigani/auth/password.py
  check_hibp(password, *, api_key=None) — if key set adds hibp-api-key
  header; key never logged.
  _check_hibp_urllib same for stdlib path.
- src/yashigani/audit/schema.py
  EventType.HIBP_API_KEY_UPDATED / HIBP_API_KEY_CLEARED.
  HibpApiKeyUpdatedEvent (masked_key_hint only, masking_applied=True).
  HibpApiKeyClearedEvent (masking_applied=True).
- src/yashigani/auth/__init__.py
  Barrel-exports: AuthSettingsStore, mask_hibp_key,
  validate_hibp_key_format, resolve_hibp_api_key, get_hibp_key_status.
- src/yashigani/backoffice/routes/__init__.py
  hibp_router import + __all__ entry.
- src/yashigani/backoffice/app.py
  Body-limit entry (/api/v1/admin/auth/hibp → 512 bytes).
  Lifespan: AuthSettingsStore wired from pool.
  include_router(hibp_router, prefix=...).
- src/yashigani/backoffice/state.py
  auth_settings_store: Optional[Any] = None field added.
- src/yashigani/backoffice/static/js/dashboard.js
  loadSettings() calls window.loadHibpStatus() if defined.
- src/yashigani/backoffice/templates/dashboard.html
  HIBP panel in #page-settings. hibp.js <script defer>.
- docs/yashigani_install_config.md
  §20.3: YASHIGANI_HIBP_API_KEY env var + admin-panel key subsection.
- docs/release-notes/v2.23.3.md
  Admin-panel HIBP API key highlights + API endpoint table.

Description: Detected a Generic API Key, potentially exposing access to various services and sensitive operations.
Author: Captain
Message:
feat(auth): HIBP password-breach check on set/change (v2.23.3)

OWASP ASVS V2.1.7: every user-chosen password change is now checked
against the Have I Been Pwned database via k-Anonymity range API.
Breached passwords are rejected with HTTP 422 + clear user message.
System-generated temp passwords are explicitly exempt (check_breach=False).

Changes:
- src/yashigani/auth/password.py
  - hibp_check_enabled(): reads YASHIGANI_HIBP_CHECK_ENABLED (default true)
  - hibp_api_url(): reads YASHIGANI_HIBP_API_URL (default pwnedpasswords.com)
  - check_hibp(): API URL read at call time (env override support); failure
    logging upgraded from DEBUG to WARNING; metric incremented on failure
  - _check_hibp_urllib(): takes api_url param (no module-level constant)
  - PasswordBreachedError: message updated to match ASVS/brief spec:
    "This password has appeared in known data breaches; choose another."
  - hash_password(): gates on hibp_check_enabled() before calling HIBP
  - validate_password_not_breached(): respects opt-out env var
- src/yashigani/auth/__init__.py — export PasswordBreachedError,
  PasswordContextError, check_hibp, validate_password_not_breached,
  hibp_check_enabled, hibp_api_url
- src/yashigani/auth/local_auth.py — check_breach=False on all
  system-generated passwords (create_admin, create_user, full_reset_user)
- src/yashigani/auth/pg_auth.py — same, 3 sites
- src/yashigani/backoffice/routes/auth.py
  - change_password: PasswordBreachedError caught before (ValueError, TypeError)
    → HTTP 422 password_breached (distinct from 400 password_rejected)
  - self_service_password_reset: temp password uses check_breach=False
- src/yashigani/metrics/registry.py — hibp_check_api_unavailable_total counter
- src/tests/unit/test_hibp.py — 46 unit tests (all GREEN)
- src/tests/integration/test_hibp_password_change.py — 6 integration tests (all GREEN)
- docs/yashigani_install_config.md § 20.3 — env-var table + network requirement
- docs/release-notes/v2.23.3.md — new release notes file

Enumerate-before-edit — hash_password call sites with no check_breach=False
(all system-generated; updated):
  grep 'hash_password(' src/yashigani/auth/local_auth.py src/yashigani/auth/pg_auth.py
  local_auth.py:94  create_admin        → check_breach=False
  local_auth.py:114 create_user         → check_breach=False
  local_auth.py:318 full_reset_user     → check_breach=False
  pg_auth.py:86     create_admin        → check_breach=False
  pg_auth.py:105    create_user         → check_breach=False
  pg_auth.py:278    full_reset_user     → check_breach=False
  local_auth.py:286 change_password     → check_breach=True (user-chosen, correct)
  pg_auth.py:247    change_password     → check_breach=True (user-chosen, correct)

Failure mode: fail-open on API unreachability — password change proceeds,
WARNING logged, metric incremented. Documented in code comments + tests + docs.

Description: PGP / GPG private key block
Author: Maxine
Message:
feat(supply-chain): GPG tag signing infrastructure (V232-N03/NEG02)

Signing method: GPG annotated tags (git tag -s) for all releases from
v2.23.1 onward. Key identity: releases@agnosticsec.com.

New files:
- scripts/sign_release_tag.sh — local signing script (offline/emergency path)
- docs/release-process.md §7–§10 — signing method, CI flow, key setup ceremony,
  retroactive signing procedure, customer-facing verification commands
- README.md §5 — "Verifying a Release" (customer-facing GPG + cosign verify)

Retroactive signing of v2.23.1 (V232-NEG02):
The CI workflow (tag-sign.yml — committed separately for PAT scope) will be
dispatched manually with tag=v2.23.1 / commit_sha=733c3624ed04bc51e1982fca690b33232861884a
once the GPG key ceremony (docs/release-process.md §9) is complete.
Tiago action required: generate key, store GPG_PRIVATE_KEY + GPG_PASSPHRASE
GitHub Secrets, then dispatch the workflow.

Finding references: V232-N03 (new signing infra), V232-NEG02 (retro-sign v2.23.1)

Description: Detected a Generic API Key, potentially exposing access to various services and sensitive operations.
Author: Maxine
Message:
fix(release): restore files dropped by -X theirs merge of v2.23.1

Recovery commit for the bad merge 1a87de7.

Background: `git merge --no-ff -X theirs 2.23.x` did not bring in files
that existed on the merge-base but were transiently-deleted on main via
the 9fa0259 → 895cfc2 revert chain. Main was left missing 30+ files
including CHANGELOG.md, .bandit, all 5 helm/templates/mtls-* files,
docker-compose.release.yml, helm values.release.yaml, scripts/release_gate_probe.sh,
scripts/archive_ci_artifacts.sh, integration tests, and others. Plus
content drift on 159 shared files.

The v2.23.1 tag at 733c362 was unaffected — operators installing from
the tag get the correct GA tree. This recovery brings the public main
branch into alignment with the GA tree.

Method: `git checkout 2.23.x -- .` overlays 2.23.x's complete tree on
main's working tree. Main-only files (none post-revert) preserved; shared
files synced; missing files restored.

History preserved per feedback_preserve_history.md — bad merge 1a87de7
remains in history as documented evidence of the SOP-5 violation
(claimed SHIPPED without evidence-bound grep against the merged tree).

Recovery option C selected by Tiago 2026-05-02 ~23:15 BST.

Author: Maxine
Message:
fix(release): restore files dropped by -X theirs merge of v2.23.1

Recovery commit for the bad merge 1a87de7.

Background: `git merge --no-ff -X theirs 2.23.x` did not bring in files
that existed on the merge-base but were transiently-deleted on main via
the 9fa0259 → 895cfc2 revert chain. Main was left missing 30+ files
including CHANGELOG.md, .bandit, all 5 helm/templates/mtls-* files,
docker-compose.release.yml, helm values.release.yaml, scripts/release_gate_probe.sh,
scripts/archive_ci_artifacts.sh, integration tests, and others. Plus
content drift on 159 shared files.

The v2.23.1 tag at 733c362 was unaffected — operators installing from
the tag get the correct GA tree. This recovery brings the public main
branch into alignment with the GA tree.

Method: `git checkout 2.23.x -- .` overlays 2.23.x's complete tree on
main's working tree. Main-only files (none post-revert) preserved; shared
files synced; missing files restored.

History preserved per feedback_preserve_history.md — bad merge 1a87de7
remains in history as documented evidence of the SOP-5 violation
(claimed SHIPPED without evidence-bound grep against the merged tree).

Description: Detected a Generic API Key, potentially exposing access to various services and sensitive operations.
Author: Tom
Message:
fix(authz): V6.8.4 step-up auth for high-value admin flows

ASVS v5 V6.8.4 — Re-authentication before critical operations.
ASVS v5 V2.4.x — Verifier impersonation resistance (app-layer gate, not
                  solely IdP-derived).

Reference: /Users/max/Documents/Claude/Internal/ACS/v3/asvs-stage-b-class3-2026-04-28.md §8.2

## What was built

New module src/yashigani/auth/stepup.py:
- has_fresh_stepup(session) — pure time check against STEPUP_TTL_SECONDS
- assert_fresh_stepup(session) — raises StepUpRequired (HTTP 401,
  detail.error="step_up_required") if not fresh
- StepUpRequired — HTTPException subclass with stepup_endpoint + ttl_seconds
- STEPUP_TTL_SECONDS — default 300s, env: YASHIGANI_STEPUP_TTL_SECONDS

Session model (auth/session.py):
- Added last_totp_verified_at: Optional[float] field (default None)
- Serialises to/from Redis (absent key → None; present → float)
- New method SessionStore.record_totp_stepup(token) → updates timestamp in Redis

Middleware (backoffice/middleware.py):
- require_stepup_admin_session() — FastAPI dependency: admin session + fresh stepup
- StepUpAdminSession — Annotated type alias for high-value route signatures

New endpoint POST /auth/stepup (auth.py):
- Accepts 6-digit TOTP code from authenticated admin
- Verifies against Postgres-backed replay cache (same as /auth/login path)
- On success: calls store.record_totp_stepup() + audit event stepup_success
- On failure: increments per-session failure counter (max 3) + audit event stepup_failure
- On exceeded: HTTP 429 + "log out and log in again" message

## High-value endpoints covered (15 total)

accounts.py:  DELETE /{username}, POST /{username}/disable, POST /{username}/force-reset
users.py:     DELETE /{username}, POST /{username}/disable, POST /{username}/full-reset
kms.py:       POST /schedule, POST /rotate-now
license.py:   POST /admin/license/activate, DELETE /admin/license
audit_sinks.py: PUT /admin/audit/siem
agents.py:    DELETE /admin/agents/{id}, POST /admin/agents/{id}/token/rotate
jwt_config.py: PUT /admin/jwt/config, DELETE /admin/jwt/config/{tenant_id}

## Admin UI (dashboard.js + dashboard.html)

New apiMutate() fetch wrapper: intercepts 401 step_up_required responses,
shows TOTP modal, POSTs to /auth/stepup, retries original request on 200.
Modal: #stepup-modal with #stepup-code input, Verify / Cancel buttons.
Enter key support in TOTP input.

High-value JS calls switched to apiMutate:
  deleteAccount(), toggleAccount(disable), deactivateAgent(), rotateAgentToken()

## Step-up TTL

Default: 300 seconds (5 minutes)
Env var: YASHIGANI_STEPUP_TTL_SECONDS

## SSO 2FA flag reconciliation

YASHIGANI_SSO_2FA_REQUIRED default flipped from "false" → "true" (sso.py:566).
Lu Stage B report noted default-off. Tiago's stated baseline: 2FA always-on.
The flag controls "force Yashigani TOTP on top of IdP SSO" — distinct from
admin local login which always requires TOTP unconditionally (no flag).
Outcome: both controls now default-on. Deploy with YASHIGANI_SSO_2FA_REQUIRED=false
to opt out of the Yashigani-on-top-of-IdP layer for SSO users.

## Tests

37 new unit tests in src/tests/unit/test_stepup_auth.py (T01–T20 + extras):
  - has_fresh_stepup: None/just-now/expired/boundary/clock-skew/under-TTL
  - assert_fresh_stepup: raises/passes
  - StepUpRequired: HTTP 401, detail fields
  - STEPUP_TTL_SECONDS config
  - Session serialisation round-trip
  - SessionStore.record_totp_stepup Redis behaviour
  - SSO 2FA flag regex check
  - Middleware AST exports
  - Failure path does not update timestamp (AST check)
  - All 15 high-value endpoints verified (AST check)
  - dashboard.js interceptor structural checks
  - /auth/stepup route structure + audit coverage

All 37 pass. 704 pre-existing unit tests unaffected.

Closes ACS-RISK-014 (V6.8.4 step-up implemented).

Description: Detected a Generic API Key, potentially exposing access to various services and sensitive operations.
Author: Maxine
Message:
feat(auth): add PostgresLocalAuthService (durable replacement)

Async Postgres-backed auth service with identical public semantics to
LocalAuthService. All AccountRecord mutations commit to the
admin_accounts table inside platform-scoped tenant_transaction blocks
so password rotations and TOTP re-enrolments survive restart.

Additions over the in-memory API:
  - get_account / get_account_by_id / list_accounts / delete_account
    replace previous private-dict lookups in the route layer.
  - set_email / force_password_change / force_totp_reprovision consolidate
    the field-mutation admin flows that previously poked AccountRecord
    fields directly.
  - set_totp_secret_direct is the INSTALLER-PRIVILEGED bootstrap-only
    path invoked from backoffice.app lifespan + routes/accounts+users
    when a pre-provisioned TOTP secret arrives out-of-band. Not for
    user-facing flows.

TOTP replay cache: verify_totp() is untouched; a proxy set hashes each
window_key with sha256 before persisting to used_totp_codes, with an
inline GC on every verify. Replay is blocked across restarts without
ever storing plaintext window keys.

LocalAuthService is intentionally preserved for tests/CLI imports.

Refs: YCS-20260423-v2.23.1-OWASP-3X P0-2
ASVS V2.1, V2.8, V11.2.4

Description: Detected a Generic API Key, potentially exposing access to various services and sensitive operations.
Author: Maxine
Message:
v2.23.1: Helm chart mTLS parity with docker-compose

Scaffolds the full core-plane mTLS mesh on the Helm umbrella chart so
K8s deployments match docker-compose v2.23.1 behaviour. Without this,
the 2.23.1 images crash-loop on Kubernetes because they expect a PKI
secret, a manifest ConfigMap, and TLS-only Redis/Postgres.

New templates:
  - mtls-rbac.yaml            ServiceAccount + Role + RoleBinding,
                              scoped to get/update/patch on the single
                              yashigani-pki-certs Secret. Empty Secret
                              pre-seeded at pre-install (-9) with
                              resource-policy: keep so RBAC doesn't
                              need create on all secrets.
  - mtls-manifest-configmap.yaml  service_identities.yaml from
                                  .Files.Get — 28 services, schema v1.
  - mtls-config-configmaps.yaml   postgres 05-enable-ssl.sh +
                                  pgbouncer.ini with hostssl/verify-ca.
  - mtls-bootstrap-job.yaml   pre-install/pre-upgrade hook (weight -5).
                              initContainer runs python -m
                              yashigani.pki.issuer bootstrap into an
                              in-memory emptyDir (medium: Memory).
                              Applier container (alpine/k8s:1.29.4)
                              kubectl-applies the leaf/intermediate/root
                              material as the PKI Secret. Empty-glob
                              guard aborts if issuer produced no files,
                              so a partial failure cannot overwrite a
                              live Secret with a stub.
  - mtls-rotation-cronjob.yaml   weekly leaf rotation with the same
                                 pattern and the same glob guard.

Service templates updated:
  - gateway/backoffice   REDIS_USE_TLS, REDIS_PORT 6380,
                         YASHIGANI_SERVICE_NAME/MANIFEST_PATH/CA_DIR,
                         DSN sslmode=verify-ca + sslcert/sslkey/sslroot,
                         BUDGET_REDIS_HOST=yashigani-budget-redis
                         (code default wouldn't resolve), python3
                         exec healthprobes using ssl + load_cert_chain
                         since httpGet can't present a client cert.
  - postgres             init ConfigMap mounted at
                         /docker-entrypoint-initdb.d/05-enable-ssl.sh
                         (mode 0555). Probe switched to pg_isready over
                         /var/run/postgresql local socket since pg_hba
                         forces hostssl+clientcert=verify-ca on TCP.
  - pgbouncer            pgbouncer.ini subPath-mounted. Probe uses
                         nc -w 2 127.0.0.1 5432 </dev/null because
                         pg_isready can't negotiate client-cert TLS.
  - redis / budget-redis TLS-only on 6380 with --tls-auth-clients yes
                         and --tls-protocols "TLSv1.2 TLSv1.3".
                         Probe uses redis-cli --tls --cert --key --cacert.
  - tests/test-redis-ping mTLS-aware redis-cli PING loop over 6380 with
                          mounted pki-certs when mtls.enabled.

values.yaml:
  - mtls: block (enabled, lifetimes, rotation schedule, secretName,
    existingSecretName, kubectlImage=alpine/k8s:1.29.4, bootstrapResources)
  - gateway image tag 2.23.1; gateway redisHost: yashigani-redis;
    opaUrl/ollamaUrl pointed at in-cluster services
  - upgrade-note documenting the postgres init-on-existing-PVC
    limitation (tracked as v2.23.2 task #54)

Pre-push review: Captain (11 items, all resolved including RBAC
tightening, kubectl image choice, postgres socket probe, pgbouncer nc
probe, redisHost fix, test-redis-ping mTLS variant), Su (2 BLOCK —
empty-glob guards, fixed), Tom (PASS with BUDGET_REDIS_HOST fix).

helm lint clean. kubectl apply --dry-run=server clean (38 resources).

Description: Detected a Generic API Key, potentially exposing access to various services and sensitive operations.
Author: Maxine
Message:
v2.23.1: Helm chart mTLS parity with docker-compose

Scaffolds the full core-plane mTLS mesh on the Helm umbrella chart so
K8s deployments match docker-compose v2.23.1 behaviour. Without this,
the 2.23.1 images crash-loop on Kubernetes because they expect a PKI
secret, a manifest ConfigMap, and TLS-only Redis/Postgres.

New templates:
  - mtls-rbac.yaml            ServiceAccount + Role + RoleBinding,
                              scoped to get/update/patch on the single
                              yashigani-pki-certs Secret. Empty Secret
                              pre-seeded at pre-install (-9) with
                              resource-policy: keep so RBAC doesn't
                              need create on all secrets.
  - mtls-manifest-configmap.yaml  service_identities.yaml from
                                  .Files.Get — 28 services, schema v1.
  - mtls-config-configmaps.yaml   postgres 05-enable-ssl.sh +
                                  pgbouncer.ini with hostssl/verify-ca.
  - mtls-bootstrap-job.yaml   pre-install/pre-upgrade hook (weight -5).
                              initContainer runs python -m
                              yashigani.pki.issuer bootstrap into an
                              in-memory emptyDir (medium: Memory).
                              Applier container (alpine/k8s:1.29.4)
                              kubectl-applies the leaf/intermediate/root
                              material as the PKI Secret. Empty-glob
                              guard aborts if issuer produced no files,
                              so a partial failure cannot overwrite a
                              live Secret with a stub.
  - mtls-rotation-cronjob.yaml   weekly leaf rotation with the same
                                 pattern and the same glob guard.

Service templates updated:
  - gateway/backoffice   REDIS_USE_TLS, REDIS_PORT 6380,
                         YASHIGANI_SERVICE_NAME/MANIFEST_PATH/CA_DIR,
                         DSN sslmode=verify-ca + sslcert/sslkey/sslroot,
                         BUDGET_REDIS_HOST=yashigani-budget-redis
                         (code default wouldn't resolve), python3
                         exec healthprobes using ssl + load_cert_chain
                         since httpGet can't present a client cert.
  - postgres             init ConfigMap mounted at
                         /docker-entrypoint-initdb.d/05-enable-ssl.sh
                         (mode 0555). Probe switched to pg_isready over
                         /var/run/postgresql local socket since pg_hba
                         forces hostssl+clientcert=verify-ca on TCP.
  - pgbouncer            pgbouncer.ini subPath-mounted. Probe uses
                         nc -w 2 127.0.0.1 5432 </dev/null because
                         pg_isready can't negotiate client-cert TLS.
  - redis / budget-redis TLS-only on 6380 with --tls-auth-clients yes
                         and --tls-protocols "TLSv1.2 TLSv1.3".
                         Probe uses redis-cli --tls --cert --key --cacert.
  - tests/test-redis-ping mTLS-aware redis-cli PING loop over 6380 with
                          mounted pki-certs when mtls.enabled.

values.yaml:
  - mtls: block (enabled, lifetimes, rotation schedule, secretName,
    existingSecretName, kubectlImage=alpine/k8s:1.29.4, bootstrapResources)
  - gateway image tag 2.23.1; gateway redisHost: yashigani-redis;
    opaUrl/ollamaUrl pointed at in-cluster services
  - upgrade-note documenting the postgres init-on-existing-PVC
    limitation (tracked as v2.23.2 task #54)

Pre-push review: Captain (11 items, all resolved including RBAC
tightening, kubectl image choice, postgres socket probe, pgbouncer nc
probe, redisHost fix, test-redis-ping mTLS variant), Su (2 BLOCK —
empty-glob guards, fixed), Tom (PASS with BUDGET_REDIS_HOST fix).

K8s deployments match docker-compose v2.23.1 behaviour. Without this,
the 2.23.1 images crash-loop on Kubernetes because they expect a PKI
secret, a manifest ConfigMap, and TLS-only Redis/Postgres.

New templates:
  - mtls-rbac.yaml            ServiceAccount + Role + RoleBinding,
                              scoped to get/update/patch on the single
                              yashigani-pki-certs Secret. Empty Secret
                              pre-seeded at pre-install (-9) with
                              resource-policy: keep so RBAC doesn't
                              need create on all secrets.
  - mtls-manifest-configmap.yaml  service_identities.yaml from
                                  .Files.Get — 28 services, schema v1.
  - mtls-config-configmaps.yaml   postgres 05-enable-ssl.sh +
                                  pgbouncer.ini with hostssl/verify-ca.
  - mtls-bootstrap-job.yaml   pre-install/pre-upgrade hook (weight -5).
                              initContainer runs python -m
                              yashigani.pki.issuer bootstrap into an
                              in-memory emptyDir (medium: Memory).
                              Applier container (alpine/k8s:1.29.4)
                              kubectl-applies the leaf/intermediate/root
                              material as the PKI Secret. Empty-glob
                              guard aborts if issuer produced no files,
                              so a partial failure cannot overwrite a
                              live Secret with a stub.
  - mtls-rotation-cronjob.yaml   weekly leaf rotation with the same
                                 pattern and the same glob guard.

Service templates updated:
  - gateway/backoffice   REDIS_USE_TLS, REDIS_PORT 6380,
                         YASHIGANI_SERVICE_NAME/MANIFEST_PATH/CA_DIR,
                         DSN sslmode=verify-ca + sslcert/sslkey/sslroot,
                         BUDGET_REDIS_HOST=yashigani-budget-redis
                         (code default wouldn't resolve), python3
                         exec healthprobes using ssl + load_cert_chain
                         since httpGet can't present a client cert.
  - postgres             init ConfigMap mounted at
                         /docker-entrypoint-initdb.d/05-enable-ssl.sh
                         (mode 0555). Probe switched to pg_isready over
                         /var/run/postgresql local socket since pg_hba
                         forces hostssl+clientcert=verify-ca on TCP.
  - pgbouncer            pgbouncer.ini subPath-mounted. Probe uses
                         nc -w 2 127.0.0.1 5432 </dev/null because
                         pg_isready can't negotiate client-cert TLS.
  - redis / budget-redis TLS-only on 6380 with --tls-auth-clients yes
                         and --tls-protocols "TLSv1.2 TLSv1.3".
                         Probe uses redis-cli --tls --cert --key --cacert.
  - tests/test-redis-ping mTLS-aware redis-cli PING loop over 6380 with
                          mounted pki-certs when mtls.enabled.

values.yaml:
  - mtls: block (enabled, lifetimes, rotation schedule, secretName,
    existingSecretName, kubectlImage=alpine/k8s:1.29.4, bootstrapResources)
  - gateway image tag 2.23.1; gateway redisHost: yashigani-redis;
    opaUrl/ollamaUrl pointed at in-cluster services
  - upgrade-note documenting the postgres init-on-existing-PVC
    limitation (tracked as v2.23.2 task #54)

Pre-push review: Captain (11 items, all resolved including RBAC
tightening, kubectl image choice, postgres socket probe, pgbouncer nc
probe, redisHost fix, test-redis-ping mTLS variant), Su (2 BLOCK —
empty-glob guards, fixed), Tom (PASS with BUDGET_REDIS_HOST fix).

helm lint clean. kubectl apply --dry-run=server clean (38 resources).

  - mtls-config-configmaps.yaml   postgres 05-enable-ssl.sh +
                                  pgbouncer.ini with hostssl/verify-ca.
  - mtls-bootstrap-job.yaml   pre-install/pre-upgrade hook (weight -5).
                              initContainer runs python -m
                              yashigani.pki.issuer bootstrap into an
                              in-memory emptyDir (medium: Memory).
                              Applier container (alpine/k8s:1.29.4)
                              kubectl-applies the leaf/intermediate/root
                              material as the PKI Secret. Empty-glob
                              guard aborts if issuer produced no files,
                              so a partial failure cannot overwrite a
                              live Secret with a stub.
  - mtls-rotation-cronjob.yaml   weekly leaf rotation with the same
                                 pattern and the same glob guard.

Service templates updated:
  - gateway/backoffice   REDIS_USE_TLS, REDIS_PORT 6380,
                         YASHIGANI_SERVICE_NAME/MANIFEST_PATH/CA_DIR,
                         DSN sslmode=verify-ca + sslcert/sslkey/sslroot,
                         BUDGET_REDIS_HOST=yashigani-budget-redis
                         (code default wouldn't resolve), python3
                         exec healthprobes using ssl + load_cert_chain
                         since httpGet can't present a client cert.
  - postgres             init ConfigMap mounted at
                         /docker-entrypoint-initdb.d/05-enable-ssl.sh
                         (mode 0555). Probe switched to pg_isready over
                         /var/run/postgresql local socket since pg_hba
                         forces hostssl+clientcert=verify-ca on TCP.
  - pgbouncer            pgbouncer.ini subPath-mounted. Probe uses
                         nc -w 2 127.0.0.1 5432 </dev/null because
                         pg_isready can't negotiate client-cert TLS.
  - redis / budget-redis TLS-only on 6380 with --tls-auth-clients yes
                         and --tls-protocols "TLSv1.2 TLSv1.3".
                         Probe uses redis-cli --tls --cert --key --cacert.
  - tests/test-redis-ping mTLS-aware redis-cli PING loop over 6380 with
                          mounted pki-certs when mtls.enabled.

Description: Detected a Generic API Key, potentially exposing access to various services and sensitive operations.
Author: Maxine
Message:
v2.23.1: Helm chart mTLS parity with docker-compose

Scaffolds the full core-plane mTLS mesh on the Helm umbrella chart so
K8s deployments match docker-compose v2.23.1 behaviour. Without this,
the 2.23.1 images crash-loop on Kubernetes because they expect a PKI
secret, a manifest ConfigMap, and TLS-only Redis/Postgres.

New templates:
  - mtls-rbac.yaml            ServiceAccount + Role + RoleBinding,
                              scoped to get/update/patch on the single
                              yashigani-pki-certs Secret. Empty Secret
                              pre-seeded at pre-install (-9) with
                              resource-policy: keep so RBAC doesn't
                              need create on all secrets.
  - mtls-manifest-configmap.yaml  service_identities.yaml from
                                  .Files.Get — 28 services, schema v1.
  - mtls-config-configmaps.yaml   postgres 05-enable-ssl.sh +
                                  pgbouncer.ini with hostssl/verify-ca.
  - mtls-bootstrap-job.yaml   pre-install/pre-upgrade hook (weight -5).
                              initContainer runs python -m
                              yashigani.pki.issuer bootstrap into an
                              in-memory emptyDir (medium: Memory).
                              Applier container (alpine/k8s:1.29.4)
                              kubectl-applies the leaf/intermediate/root
                              material as the PKI Secret. Empty-glob
                              guard aborts if issuer produced no files,
                              so a partial failure cannot overwrite a
                              live Secret with a stub.
  - mtls-rotation-cronjob.yaml   weekly leaf rotation with the same
                                 pattern and the same glob guard.

Service templates updated:
  - gateway/backoffice   REDIS_USE_TLS, REDIS_PORT 6380,
                         YASHIGANI_SERVICE_NAME/MANIFEST_PATH/CA_DIR,
                         DSN sslmode=verify-ca + sslcert/sslkey/sslroot,
                         BUDGET_REDIS_HOST=yashigani-budget-redis
                         (code default wouldn't resolve), python3
                         exec healthprobes using ssl + load_cert_chain
                         since httpGet can't present a client cert.
  - postgres             init ConfigMap mounted at
                         /docker-entrypoint-initdb.d/05-enable-ssl.sh
                         (mode 0555). Probe switched to pg_isready over
                         /var/run/postgresql local socket since pg_hba
                         forces hostssl+clientcert=verify-ca on TCP.
  - pgbouncer            pgbouncer.ini subPath-mounted. Probe uses
                         nc -w 2 127.0.0.1 5432 </dev/null because
                         pg_isready can't negotiate client-cert TLS.
  - redis / budget-redis TLS-only on 6380 with --tls-auth-clients yes
                         and --tls-protocols "TLSv1.2 TLSv1.3".
                         Probe uses redis-cli --tls --cert --key --cacert.
  - tests/test-redis-ping mTLS-aware redis-cli PING loop over 6380 with
                          mounted pki-certs when mtls.enabled.

    limitation (tracked as v2.23.2 task #54)
